entities.name: Layer 0 — Contracts
entities.what_it_solves: Typed Pydantic models that every engine reads/writes — the API surface between engines.
entities.how_it_solves: PatientState, SomaticMutation, CaseResolution, RankedIntervention, RSFeatureSet are versioned dataclasses/Pydantic models. Every engine boundary is a contract transition; nothing crosses layers as loose dicts.
entities.why_it_exists: Prevents drift: if two engines share a field name, they share the model. No 'stringly-typed' RS scores.

entities.name: Layer 1 — Engines
entities.what_it_solves: Domain engines encapsulate a single question (e.g. synthetic lethality, lineage dependency).
entities.how_it_solves: SL engine lives in core/, multimodal/, kb/, audit/, api/ submodules with strict internal boundaries. Lineage Dependency Engine is PLANNED (Sprint C) — not built.
entities.why_it_exists: Isolates model logic from orchestration so engines can be unit-tested in the R&D silo before promotion.

entities.name: Layer 3 — Orchestrator
entities.what_it_solves: The 12-engine pipeline that composes contract-typed engines into a case resolution.
entities.how_it_solves: SL is Phase 3.5 in the pipeline. Entry point: Orchestrator.resolve_case(patient_state) -> CaseResolution.
entities.why_it_exists: Single choke-point for cross-cutting invariants (RUO, audit trail, tier clamps).

entities.name: Layer 4 — Output
entities.what_it_solves: HTTP routes and Ayesha bundle assembly for downstream consumers.
entities.how_it_solves: compat_routes + ayesha_bundle_routes; canonical case endpoint POST /api/v1/case/resolve.
entities.why_it_exists: Backward-compat for legacy clients + typed bundle for the new front-end without duplicating engine logic.

entities.name: Ayesha bundle backend waterfall (7 stages)
entities.what_it_solves: Composes an end-to-end therapy-fit bundle for the front-end by chaining safety, efficacy, IO, trials, SOC, biomarker services.
entities.how_it_solves: Sequential waterfall: each stage reads the accumulated bundle, gates on prior outputs (e.g. PGx safety before IO recommendation), and appends its own typed subsection.
entities.why_it_exists: Removes duplicated cross-service logic from Layer 4 routes and centralises the ordering rules (safety-before-efficacy, mechanism-before-trial-match) that CRISPRO_HARDENING_AUDIT flagged as leaky in v2.

entities.name: SL frontend canonicalization + Ayesha page merge
entities.what_it_solves: Merges duplicated SL/Ayesha pages into a single canonical view backed by typed engine output; removes call-path duplicates.
entities.how_it_solves: Enumerated in SL_FRONTEND_CANONICALIZATION.md, AYESHA_PAGE_MERGE_REPORT.md, CALL_PATH_DEDUP_REPORT.md, CONFIG_NORMALIZATION_REPORT.md, HYGIENE_DELETE_PROOF.md — each report lists deleted files with reasons.

entities.name: MRTX1719 → WRN axis mismap (OPEN)
entities.what_it_solves: pharmacologic_analyzer maps MRTX1719 to the WRN axis, but MRTX1719 is a PRMT5/MTAP-cooperative agent — the WRN axis is wrong.
entities.how_it_solves: Current code: `'mrtx1719': 'wrn'` at pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66. Correct mapping per Bev2026 + Engstrom 2023 (Cancer Discov 13:2412-2431) + Pettus 2025 (J Med Chem 68:6932-6954): `'mrtx1719': 'prmt5_mtap_cooperative'` — but that axis does not exist yet in the CandidateAxis enum.
entities.why_it_exists: Silent axis mislabelling propagates through the SL engine's pharmacology contribution; any downstream tier decision referencing MRTX1719 for WRN is currently wrong.

source_receipts.note: bug location — not in this KB corpus

gaps_still_open: Fix requires (1) adding PRMT5_MTAP_COOPERATIVE axis to the enum, (2) re-tiering, (3) regression tests, (4) canonical receipt for the new axis.

entities.name: MBD4 × ATR/WEE1 evidence matrix (v2)
entities.what_it_solves: The canonical evidence receipt for the MBD4 × ATR/WEE1 axis — the highest-stakes claim CrisPRO makes for this bundle.
entities.how_it_solves: Structured JSON with one modality_cell per evidence source (CRISPR co-dependency, CRISPR MSI-stratified, PRISM, GDSC2, TCGA-OV, UCEC survival, pan-cancer LOF, dependency probability). Each cell has a verdict + why-it-does-not-support-STRONG. The overall_verdict block is the ground truth for the tier statement.
entities.why_it_exists: Prevents any downstream summariser from silently upgrading MBD4 × ATR/WEE1 to STRONG. Every module reads the receipt, not memory.

overall_verdict.justification: All 5 computational modalities return NULL or MISSING. The one marginal signal (TCGA-OV RS score Wilcoxon p=0.016) uses expression as a proxy and does not survive Spearman correlation. MBD4-LOF is too rare in all public datasets (0.19% pan-cancer, 1.02% in DepMap) to power any statistical test. No published clinical trial has tested ATRi/WEE1i in MBD4-LOF patients.
overall_verdict.fundamental_limitation: MBD4 is a DNA repair gene — its LOF cannot be reliably proxied by expression level. The correct experimental model (isogenic MBD4-KO) is not represented in any public dataset.
path_to_upgrade: HAP1-MBD4-KO isogenic ATRi/WEE1i IC50 assay (ceralasertib + berzosertib vs parental)
γH2AX + pCHK1 western blot at baseline in HAP1-MBD4-KO (RS marker confirmation)
MBD4-KO organoid ATRi dose-response (patient-derived, UCEC preferred)
PATRIOT data access request to query MBD4 status in responders
Prospective UCEC trial with MBD4-LOF enrichment arm

tier_upgrade_assessment.upgrade_rule: BH-adjusted p < 0.05 AND Cohen's d < -0.5 for A6 Group A vs Group B (ATR)
tier_upgrade_assessment.rationale: Upgrade criterion NOT met. A6 ATR A vs B: padj=0.229 (>0.05), Cohen's d=-0.129 (>-0.5). A7 is DESCRIPTIVE (n=2 LOF, no inferential test, inconsistent RS direction). A8 is NEGATIVE (pan-essentiality ceiling effect, CHEK1/WEE1 wrong direction). Overall tier remains: Mechanistic candidate only.

verbatim_evidence.context: overall_verdict.tier_change value — canonical answer to 'did the tier change?'
target_tier top-level field — what upgrade WOULD be, not what is.

gaps_still_open: MBD4-LOF cannot be reliably proxied by expression level (fundamental limitation noted in overall_verdict).
No clinical readout: no ATRi/WEE1i trial has published MBD4-LOF stratified data.
CRISPR MBD4-LOF vs WT contrast is confounded by MSI-H collinearity.

attributes.document_purpose: Compare CrisPRO's methodological 'moat' against Bevilacqua 2026's published approach + adjacent literature, with every claim traceable to PDF, transcript receipts, or paper references.

primary_source.paper: Bevilacqua R, Gasperini P, Cantore T, et al. Therapeutic vulnerabilities exposed by the 9p21 loss identified through multiparametric drug screening inform rational combination strategies.
primary_source.retrieval_method: Full PDF read (16 pages) via Read tool on /mnt/user-uploads/s41698-026-01434-w.pdf, 2026-06-24
primary_source.code_availability: Not publicly available; available on reasonable request from corresponding authors per paper Code Availability statement

provenance.verification_status: Every leaf below is traceable to a specific page/figure/table of the source PDF or to a verifiable transcript receipt. No claim retained from prior generated JSONs unless re-verified.
audit_corrections_applied: MTAP-loss prevalence in TCGA MIBC = 26% (paper text, Discussion §1, cBioPortal computation by the authors). 22% in PLAN.md was the older Robertson 2017 CDKN2A-alone figure for MIBC; both retained, distinguished.

Phenotypic features = 12 (verbatim, Results §Phenotype-based hit selection: 'For each object (i.e., cell or nucleus), 12 phenotypic features...'). PLAN.md had hedged this to 'feature count not in retrieved supplementary'; PDF resolves it definitively to 12.
SynergyFinder version = 3.1 (verbatim, Fig 3 legend). Reference 21 (Ianevski 2022) is the SynergyFinder 3.0 paper; the authors used the 3.1 release derived from the same software lineage.
ATR inhibitor name verbatim = VX970 (no hyphen, no 'berzosertib'). Paper uses VX970 throughout.

Compound library composition: 2,349 total = 349 (Selleck Anti-cancer) + 958 (MicroSource Spectrum known drugs in human therapy) + 629 (natural products) + 343 (biological activity at experimental level) + 70 (approved for agricultural use only). Numerical sum = 2,349.

PDO clinical context: BlCa197 = 54-year-old female, high-grade MIBC, mixed urothelial+sarcomatoid, neoadjuvant cisplatin/gemcitabine + radical cystectomy. TN-001 = 53-year-old male, high-grade MIBC, mixed urothelial+sarcomatoid, MVAC combination + radical cystectomy.
Ethical approvals: KEK 06/03 and 2017-02295.
AZD7762 and MK-1775 z-scores from primary screen now stated verbatim: AZD7762 WT z=-3.60 / 3KO z=-5.06; MK-1775 WT z=-1.12 / 3KO z=-2.06.

MRTX1719 axis bug in repo: pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66 maps 'mrtx1719' -> 'wrn'. Correct axis per Bev2026 §Methods +  Engstrom 2023 ref 7 = PRMT5 (MTA-cooperative). Flag in DH JSON; no code fix in this task per user lock #4.

attributes.definition_of_moat: Defensible methodological features that distinguish CrisPRO's approach from established or emerging competitors. Moat ≠ moral superiority; moat = features competitors cannot easily replicate without comparable infrastructure, expertise, or data access.

where_CrisPRO_genuinely_outperforms: Computational falsification at scale (PARP1 axis in MBD4-LOF context demonstrated; methodology generalizable)
Systematic confound stress-testing within single dataset (MSI + TP53 + LOO + lineage)
Open code + artifact provenance with full-precision statistical values
Cross-axis evidence matrix that survives compaction (canonical artifact frozen 2026-04-05)
where_Bev2026_outperforms: Cellular validation in isogenic pairs + cross-tumor + PDOs (gold-standard biological evidence)
Bi-directional MTAP editing (rescue + KO) as in-cell falsification mechanism
Wet-lab synergy measurement (SynergyFinder 3.1 HSA scores with 95% CI)
honest_moat_assessment.complementarity_opportunity: CrisPRO + Bev2026 lab = computational triage + cellular validation. CrisPRO ranks candidate PDOs by biomarker score → Demichelis/Kruithof-de Julio lab validates the top-N in wet lab. Each side does what it cannot do alone.

The statement 'Open code + artifact provenance' remains true at the file-existence level, but the assertion that those artifacts represent verified-reproducible work is not yet substantiated.

entities.name: Falsification arm methodology
entities.what_it_solves: Falsification arm methodology
entities.how_it_solves: PARP1 expression Mann-Whitney falsification arm: MBD4-LOF vs comparator (n=19/1498), median LOF 6.770 vs comparator 6.656, p=0.605 (two-sided). Sanity check: pan-cancer PARP1×PARPi ρ=-0.416, p=1.36e-21, n=481. Verifies that PARP-trapping mechanism is NOT operative in MBD4-LOF and pivots toward ATR axis as recommended therapy.

attributes.moat_status: COMPLEMENTARY. Bev2026's wet-lab 2KO falsification is gold-standard but constrained to one gene at a time. CrisPRO's in-silico Mann-Whitney falsification scales across 18,000+ genes × multiple axes.
attributes.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF §Results + Supp Fig 2D. CrisPRO from user-provided 'precise numbers' message transcript line 12288 (verbatim).

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach: 2KO clones (CDKN2A/2B deleted, MTAP retained) used as in-cell falsification arm. If sensitivity is MTAP-driven, 2KO should NOT respond to PRMT5/MAT2A inhibitors → confirmed (Supp Fig 2B, 2D).
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_evidence_quality: Strong - actual cellular falsification in same assay system.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: PARP1 expression Mann-Whitney falsification arm: MBD4-LOF vs comparator (n=19/1498), median LOF 6.770 vs comparator 6.656, p=0.605 (two-sided). Sanity check: pan-cancer PARP1×PARPi ρ=-0.416, p=1.36e-21, n=481. Verifies that PARP-trapping mechanism is NOT operative in MBD4-LOF and pivots toward ATR axis as recommended therapy.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_evidence_quality: In-silico falsification using public DepMap expression data + pan-cancer pharmacology Spearman correlation. Differs from Bev2026 in being computational not cellular, but enables falsification of competing hypotheses at scale across the full gene-axis matrix.

attributes.moat_status: CrisPRO ADVANTAGE for systematic confound testing across stratifiers. Bev2026 validates across systems; CrisPRO validates across statistical stratifiers within one dataset. Both are necessary, but CrisPRO's approach can be applied PRE-publication to triage hypotheses.
attributes.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF §Results + Supp Fig 1A, 1F, 6A. CrisPRO from canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json frozen 2026-04-05, full-precision JSON in transcript indices 991/1010 + user-provided 'precise numbers' message line 12288.

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach: (a) Test in 2 independent isogenic clone pairs per cell line (Fig 1F, 1G) to exclude clone-selection artifact. (b) Test in T24 (second cell line) and PDOs (2 patient sources) to exclude cell-line artifact. (c) Test in pancreatic adenocarcinoma + mesothelioma to exclude lineage artifact.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_evidence_quality: Strong cellular validation across systems but no formal stratifier analysis with explicit n breakdowns.
CrisPRO_approach_with_receipts: Stress #1 (MSI purge): exclude ModelSubtypeFeatures containing 'MSI' from both arms. Effect STRENGTHENS: n_LOF=10/n_WT=906, Δ=-0.9095277046357615, p=0.015328932966132268, d=-0.6227047947387747. Rules out: MSI-H as confounder.

Stress #2 (TP53 stratification): MBD4-LOF∩TP53-mut vs MBD4-WT(Methods)∩TP53-mut. Effect is LARGEST: n_LOF=11/n_WT=619, LN_IC50 Δ=-1.0692019443383756, p=0.003002668797799231, d=-0.7404782024497254. AUC Δ=-0.08694390659421358, p=0.0008734372333767206, d=-0.8888045345020856. Rules out: TP53 dependency; relevant because >95% of HGSOC is TP53-mutant.
Stress #3 (Leave-one-out × 14): Max p across 14 iterations = 0.045165724128583974. All 14/14 iterations p<0.05. Rules out: Single-line driver.

Stress #4 (Non-bowel lineage): n_LOF=9/n_WT=873, Δ=-0.8706814558991984, p=0.02533035508952329, d=-0.5988100454283156. Rules out: Bowel-lineage artifact (bowel-only n_LOF=5, p=0.12638007986845198 underpowered).
Primary endpoint (Methods_no_somatic_MBD4 rule, ceralasertib LN_IC50): n_LOF=14, n_WT=914, Δ=-0.7324529879649893, p=0.021484496737088882, d=-0.5032867186922607.

entities.what_it_solves: Patient-derived organoid (PDO) deployment

attributes.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on translational relevance. CrisPRO can offer pre-screening prioritization to Demichelis/Kruithof-de Julio PDO biobanks before they commit organoid resource.

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach: Two PDOs from Inselspital Bern (BlCa197) + Spitalzentrum Biel (TN-001), both high-grade MIBC mixed urothelial/sarcomatoid. Bi-directional editing: TN-001 doxycycline-inducible MTAP rescue + BlCa197 MTAP-KO induction. CellTiter-Glo 3D readout. n=3 biological replicates × 2 technical replicates per drug condition.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_validation: MTAP-OE in 3KO PDO RESCUES MRTX1719 activity; MTAP-KO in WT PDO INCREASES MRTX1719 activity. Bi-directional rescue is a powerful validation.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: No primary PDO data — relies on DepMap CCLE cell lines (n=1186) + GDSC2 drug screens. Public-data dependent.
raw_dimension_record.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on translational relevance. CrisPRO can offer pre-screening prioritization to Demichelis/Kruithof-de Julio PDO biobanks before they commit organoid resource.
raw_dimension_record.complementary_value: CrisPRO's biomarker-stratification triage + Kruithof-de Julio's TOR organoid bank = mutually beneficial pipeline.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_PDO_clinical_history_verbatim: PDO lines, TN-001 and BlCa197, were generated from specimens obtained from patients who underwent cystectomy at the Inselspital, University Hospital, in Bern, or Spitalzentrum Biel, respectively. Specifically, BlCa197 cells were isolated from a 54-year-old female patient with high-grade MIBC with mixed urothelial and sarcomatoid histology. This patient underwent neoadjuvant cisplatin/gemcitabine treatment followed by radical cystectomy. TN-001 was obtained from a 53-year-old male patient diagnosed with high-grade MIBC with mixed urothelial and sarcomatoid histology. This patient received MVAC combination followed by radical cystectomy.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_PDO_clinical_history_source: Bev2026 PDF, Methods section 'Clinical details of patient-derived organoid lines' (page 14)
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_bidirectional_editing_verbatim: To obtain TN-001 PDOs with doxycycline-inducible MTAP overexpression, the MTAP gene was cloned into pCW57.1 (Addgene #41393), which was then used to transduce TN-001 cells. For MTAP knockout in BlCa197 PDOs, MTAP sgRNA sequence was cloned into a lentiCRISPR_v1 plasmid as previously described, which was then used to transduce BlCa197 cells.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_PDO_rescue_finding_verbatim: MTAP overexpression in TN-001 organoids rescues MRTX1719 activity, while MTAP KO selectively increases MRTX1719 activity in BlCa197 (Supplementary Fig. 7A).
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_PDO_rescue_finding_source: Bev2026 PDF, Results 'Combination of cytarabine and VX970 with MRTX1719 is effective in BLCA patient-derived organoids' (page 8)

entities.name: Synergy quantification methodology
entities.what_it_solves: Synergy quantification methodology
entities.how_it_solves: CrisPRO does not currently perform wet-lab synergy quantification — relies on GDSC2 single-agent IC50 / AUC + DepMap CRISPR dependency for axis ranking.
entities.why_it_exists: Encoded gap between CrisPRO methodology and Bev2026.

attributes.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on wet-lab synergy measurement methodology. CrisPRO can offer in-silico drug-combination ranking via GDSC2 axis pairing (e.g., ATR-inhibitor × cytidine-analog combination predictions for MBD4-LOF), but does not generate primary synergy scores.
attributes.Bev2026_approach_verbatim: Synergy was calculated with two models (HSA and Bliss) using SynergyFinder 3.1, and HSA synergy scores and summary synergy scores with 95% confidence interval for the CCK-8 experiments are shown.

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach_verbatim: Synergy was calculated with two models (HSA and Bliss) using SynergyFinder 3.1, and HSA synergy scores and summary synergy scores with 95% confidence interval for the CCK-8 experiments are shown.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_software_note: Paper uses SynergyFinder 3.1, but cites reference 21 (Ianevski et al. 2022 Nucleic Acids Res 50:W739-W743) which is the SynergyFinder 3.0 publication. The 3.1 release apparently postdates the original publication and is referenced by version number only in the figure caption.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: CrisPRO does not currently perform wet-lab synergy quantification — relies on GDSC2 single-agent IC50 / AUC + DepMap CRISPR dependency for axis ranking.
raw_dimension_record.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on wet-lab synergy measurement methodology. CrisPRO can offer in-silico drug-combination ranking via GDSC2 axis pairing (e.g., ATR-inhibitor × cytidine-analog combination predictions for MBD4-LOF), but does not generate primary synergy scores.
raw_dimension_record.complementary_value: If CrisPRO computational ranking nominates a combination, Bev2026's published HSA/Bliss methodology with 95% CI is the reproducible gold-standard for wet-lab validation.

The 22% figure (CDKN2A-alone in MIBC, Robertson 2017) is also cited as a complementary endpoint when MTAP-status is not specified. raw_dimension_record.dimension: Source of MTAP-loss MIBC prevalence statistic
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach_verbatim_1: Our analysis of the TCGA MIBC cohort showed that approximately 26% of patients harbor homozygous deletion of CDKN2A/CDKN2B/MTAP, while only a minority (approximately 5%) harbor homozygous deletions of only CDKN2A/2B.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach_verbatim_2: Co-occurrence of CDKN2A/2B and MTAP deletions interests 26% of TCGA MIBC patients (p value < 0.001 based on...
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach_verbatim_3: Several studies have investigated the molecular landscape of NMIBC and MIBC, revealing that CDKN2A loss at chromosome 9p21 is the most common copy number alteration in BLCA, occurring in approximately 42 and 22% of NMIBC and MIBC, respectively13,14.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: Reproduces and cites Bev2026's 26% figure for MTAP/CDKN2A/CDKN2B co-deletion. The 22% figure (CDKN2A-alone in MIBC, Robertson 2017) is also cited as a complementary endpoint when MTAP-status is not specified. CrisPRO has NOT independently reproduced either number — both are taken from external citations.
raw_dimension_record.moat_status: NEUTRAL — both numbers are Bev2026 + Robertson/Lindskrog original findings. CrisPRO cites them appropriately.
raw_dimension_record.attribution_clarity: Important: 26% (Bev2026 = MTAP+CDKN2A/B co-deletion) ≠ 22% (Robertson 2017 = CDKN2A loss alone, MTAP status not specified). Conflating them would be a methodological error.
raw_dimension_record.verification_status: All three verbatim quotes confirmed by direct PDF read 2026-06-24 (pages 2, 12 of PDF). Specific pages cited per quote.

entities.name: Synthetic Lethality Engine

entities.why_it_exists: Every axis dependency previously had scattered, ad-hoc logic; the engine consolidates fusion + tiering + provenance in one testable module with strict boundaries.

entities.name: Case Resolution Orchestrator (12-engine pipeline, SL is Phase 3.5)

entities.why_it_exists: Single choke-point for cross-cutting concerns: audit trail, RUO middleware, tier clamps, POLE guard, underpowered guard.

entities.name: VALIDATED
entities.what_it_solves: Tier 'VALIDATED' in the 4-level SL evidence hierarchy.
entities.how_it_solves: Never auto-assigned by the fuser; only reachable via source == 'frozen_receipt' (invariant #1).

attributes.entry_criterion: Highest tier. Score ≥ 10 AND clinical == POSITIVE.

entities.name: STRONG
entities.what_it_solves: Tier 'STRONG' in the 4-level SL evidence hierarchy.
entities.how_it_solves: Reachable by modality fuser OR by rs_promotion() when RS_high and independent evidence.
entities.why_it_exists: Threshold captures 'multiple independent lines of positive evidence' without demanding clinical readouts.

entities.name: MECHANISTIC
entities.what_it_solves: Tier 'MECHANISTIC' in the 4-level SL evidence hierarchy.
entities.how_it_solves: Default fallback when only mechanistic reasoning or a single low-weight modality supports the axis.
entities.why_it_exists: Explicitly under-claims: signals interest without pretending to have fusion power.

entities.name: NOT_SUPPORTED / INSUFFICIENT
entities.what_it_solves: Tier 'NOT_SUPPORTED / INSUFFICIENT' in the 4-level SL evidence hierarchy.
entities.how_it_solves: Terminal 'refuse to recommend' state.
entities.why_it_exists: Failure closed: the SL engine returns absence-of-evidence explicitly rather than surfacing a low-confidence recommendation.

entities.name: clinical
entities.what_it_solves: Evidence modality 'clinical' consumed by the multimodal fuser.
entities.how_it_solves: Contributes weight 4 to the fused score. Positive/negative/null verdicts are aggregated with signed contributions.
entities.why_it_exists: Weights are hard-coded to reflect scientific hierarchy — clinical evidence dominates cell-line signals; CRISPR + expression are supporting only.

entities.name: in_vivo
entities.what_it_solves: Evidence modality 'in_vivo' consumed by the multimodal fuser.
entities.how_it_solves: Contributes weight 3 to the fused score. Positive/negative/null verdicts are aggregated with signed contributions.
entities.why_it_exists: Weights are hard-coded to reflect scientific hierarchy — clinical evidence dominates cell-line signals; CRISPR + expression are supporting only.

entities.how_it_solves: Contributes weight 2 to the fused score. Positive/negative/null verdicts are aggregated with signed contributions.
entities.why_it_exists: Weights are hard-coded to reflect scientific hierarchy — clinical evidence dominates cell-line signals; CRISPR + expression are supporting only.

entities.name: crispr

entities.name: Multimodal Fuser
entities.what_it_solves: Computes the fused evidence score per (patient, axis) that the tier logic consumes.
entities.how_it_solves: Sums signed weight contributions across 7 modalities. Positive verdict → +weight, negative → -weight, null → 0. Distinct positive sources are counted for the STRONG threshold (≥ 2 distinct positives).
entities.why_it_exists: Encodes 'scientific hierarchy' explicitly: a positive clinical signal outweighs three positive CRISPR hits; cell-line data cannot override clinical.

entities.name: Replication Stress (RS) Framework
entities.what_it_solves: Composite RS_score built from 6 binary features that classifies a tumor as RS_high / RS_med / RS_low.
entities.how_it_solves: rs_score = sum(feature_weight if feature else 0). Bands: high ≥ 0.40, medium ≥ 0.20, low < 0.20. RS_high tumors are eligible for rs_promotion on RS-sensitive axes.
entities.why_it_exists: Encodes 'this tumor has enough replication stress to make checkpoint inhibition rational' as a first-class, per-patient feature — instead of scattering CCNE1/MYC/TP53 checks across engines.

entities.name: RS-based Tier Promotion
entities.what_it_solves: Lifts MECHANISTIC axis results to STRONG when the tumor is RS_high AND the axis is RS-sensitive AND independent evidence exists.
entities.how_it_solves: See attributes.code_verbatim — canonical function from CRISPRO_SL_PHD_ROADMAP Ch 3 with explicit early-exits at every guard.
entities.why_it_exists: Encodes the domain claim 'in a high-replication-stress background, a mechanistic hint on an RS-sensitive checkpoint is worth more'. Guards prevent it from over-writing VALIDATED / STRONG or from firing on non-RS axes.

entities.name: VALIDATED never auto-assigned
entities.what_it_solves: Non-negotiable engine rule: 'VALIDATED never auto-assigned'
entities.how_it_solves: Fuser code refuses to output tier=VALIDATED. Only source == 'frozen_receipt' assignments can reach VALIDATED.
entities.why_it_exists: Blocks the pipeline from ever claiming clinical-grade evidence without a signed frozen receipt.

entities.name: Tier clamp: max STRONG without clinical POSITIVE
entities.what_it_solves: Non-negotiable engine rule: 'Tier clamp: max STRONG without clinical POSITIVE'
entities.how_it_solves: Post-fuser clamp: if no modality with weight==4 (clinical) returned POSITIVE, final tier is capped at STRONG.
entities.why_it_exists: Prevents 'STRONG cell-line + STRONG in-vivo → VALIDATED' short-circuits.

entities.name: Receipt immutability (deepcopy on read)
entities.what_it_solves: Non-negotiable engine rule: 'Receipt immutability (deepcopy on read)'
entities.how_it_solves: All frozen-receipt reads deepcopy the KB row before returning to the caller.
entities.why_it_exists: Downstream mutation of a receipt can never propagate back into the KB or into other axes' results.

entities.name: KB read-only direction
entities.what_it_solves: Non-negotiable engine rule: 'KB read-only direction'
entities.how_it_solves: Engines have read handles into the KB; write access is confined to admin ingestion scripts, not the API pipeline.
entities.why_it_exists: Blocks accidental production writes from a live case resolution.

entities.name: POLE guard (POLE hotspot → msi_h=False)
entities.what_it_solves: Non-negotiable engine rule: 'POLE guard (POLE hotspot → msi_h=False)'
entities.how_it_solves: If a canonical POLE hotspot mutation is detected in PatientState, RSFeatureSet.MSI_H is forced to False regardless of upstream flags.
entities.why_it_exists: POLE-mutant hypermutation has been misclassified as MSI-H in upstream tools; this guard prevents WRN axis being triggered on a POLE-only case.

entities.name: RUO on all responses
entities.what_it_solves: Non-negotiable engine rule: 'RUO on all responses'
entities.how_it_solves: Every CaseResolution response is wrapped by RUO middleware: ruo=True + disclaimer='Research use only...'
entities.why_it_exists: Non-negotiable regulatory posture — no clinician-facing output ever ships without the RUO badge.

entities.name: AuditEntry per tier assignment
entities.what_it_solves: Non-negotiable engine rule: 'AuditEntry per tier assignment'
entities.how_it_solves: Each RankedIntervention has an AuditEntry recording tier, source, promotion_source, and the exact modality contributions.
entities.why_it_exists: Full traceability: given a response, you can reconstruct why an axis got the tier it did.

entities.name: Underpowered guard (n_mut < 15 → PromotionBlockedError)
entities.what_it_solves: Non-negotiable engine rule: 'Underpowered guard (n_mut < 15 → PromotionBlockedError)'
entities.how_it_solves: If the reference cohort for a promotion decision has n < 15, apply_rs_promotion raises PromotionBlockedError instead of promoting.
entities.why_it_exists: Stops the fuser from claiming 'STRONG (rs_promotion)' when the underlying cohort is statistically underpowered.

entities.how_it_solves: Tier assignment for MBD4/cytidine axis was demoted to correct level; VALIDATED entry constrained to frozen_receipt.
entities.why_it_exists: Prior sprints shipped the SL engine without these hardening rules; Sprint 4S closed the identified holes.

hits.text: **Key finding for Brenus/STC-1010 positioning:** The backend already contains a `brenus_diligence/` module with hardcoded guardrails, flag enforcement, and a canon-frozen blocker registry specifically for the BreAK CRC-001 / STC-1010 diligence package. This is the most important discovery — CrisPRO has already internalized the Brenus use case at the code level.

```
crispro-backend-v2/
├── api/                          # FastAPI application (primary)
│   ├── main.py                   # App factory + 40+ router registrations
│   ├── config.py                 # Env config, model routing, feature flags
│   ├── routers/                  # 40+ endpoint modules
│   ├── services/                 # Business logic (100+ service modules)
│   ├── schemas/                  # Pydantic request/response models
│   ├── resources/                # Static data (disease priors, trial vectors, SAE maps)
│   └── middleware/               # Auth, HIPAA PII, MFA, quota, security headers
├── brenus_diligence/             # *** Brenus-specific guardrails (CRITICAL) ***
├── biomarker_enriched_cohorts/   # TCGA/cBioPortal enriched cohort data + validation
├── cohort_validation/            # DDR-bin survival validation scripts
├── dosing_guidance_validation/   # CPIC concordance validation
├── governance/formula_decision/  # PATH A decision record (signed 2026-04-28)
├── knowledge_base/               # Structured KB (genes, variants, pathways, cohorts)
├── Pubmed-LLM-Agent-main/        # RAG-based PubMed agent (embedded)
├── scripts/                      # Benchmarking, data acquisition, analysis scripts
└── evidence/sl_full_context_audit/ # Live SL audit artifacts (Cases A–D)
```
This module is directly wired into the backend and represents the MBD4 manuscript findings in production code.
### 2.5 Brenus Diligence Module (`brenus_diligence/`) — CRITICAL
This is the most strategically important discovery. The backend has a dedicated Brenus module:

**Canon source:** `brenus_canon_v2_freeze.md v2.1-FREEZE (2026-04-28)`  
**Blocker source:** `brenus_final_blockers_registry.md v1.1-FINAL (2026-04-28)`

| Gap | Severity | Details |
|-----|----------|---------|
| No human efficacy data for STC-1010 | HARD_BLOCK (B-003) | BreAK CRC-001 hasResults=false; n=6 preliminary signals from press releases only |
| HT29 BRAF V600E conflict | ACTIVE_CONFLICT (SC-001) | BRAF-mutant cell line in vaccine for BRAF-WT patients — unresolved |
| No CPI in regimen | HARD_BLOCK | Regimen confirmed: STC-1010 + low-dose Cy + GM-CSF + mFOLFOX6 ± bev; no ICI |
| DL-07 Figure 2 DDR 0.983 | QUARANTINED | Mathematically impossible under PATH A ceiling (0.8898); publication-blocking |
| LATIFY deltas (CT-03/DL-05/PC-06) | QUARANTINED | Vector version unresolved |
| PC-02 | PERMANENTLY DOWNGRADED | Reproducibility failure; no recomputation will change this |
*Report generated from verbatim GitHub API fetch. All findings are from source code inspection, not inference.*

hits.text: 3. **`useAyeshaCareData` passes `options` to `generatePlan` as a merged object** — the `buildRequest` function in `useAyeshaProfile` merges options, but `generatePlan` then does `{ ...baseRequest, ...options }` which means the options object (which includes `include_*` flags) **overwrites** the base request's flags. If `options` is missing a flag, it won't be set to `true` even if `buildRequestFromProfile` set it. This is a silent data loss bug.

**Critical difference:** The orchestrator's SL call omits `hgvs_p` for TP53 (IHC-only evidence) and sends a minimal payload. The `useSyntheticLethality` hook sends full coordinates for TP53 (the RUO proxy hotspot). These will produce different SL results.

- [ ] **Parallelize `complete_care_v2` backend** — wrap independent services (SOC, CA-125, food, resistance) in `asyncio.gather`
- [ ] **Add timeout guards** — `asyncio.wait_for(service_call(), timeout=10.0)` on each service in orchestrator
- [ ] **Axis B gate in UI** — show "Pending NGS" badge on IO/hypermutator SL outputs when TMB is null
- [ ] **Batch essentiality endpoint** — replace per-gene loop with single batch call
- [ ] **Fix `useAyeshaCareData` options merge bug** — ensure `include_*` flags from `buildRequestFromProfile` are not silently overwritten

curl https://crispro-backend-v2.onrender.com/debug/cors
# Must return JSON with "https://crispro-front-end.onrender.com" in allowed_origins list
```

This cost ~3 hours of debugging. The Render service runs:
```
uvicorn api.index:app
```
NOT `uvicorn main:app` (what `Procfile` and `render.yaml` say). The Render dashboard `startCommand` overrides both.

The backend is on Render's starter plan and hibernates. First request after hibernation returns `503` with header `x-render-routing: hibernate-wake-error`. This is not a code bug — retry after 5-10 seconds. The frontend should implement retry logic for this.

### 4E. `/debug/cors` endpoint needs to be removed
Commit `429e3ac5` added a `/debug/cors` endpoint that exposes the allowed origins list. Fine for debugging, should be removed before any public launch. One-line delete in `api/index.py`.

hits.text:
# CrisPRO Frontend Audit & Fix Plan
**Repo:** `fjkiani/crispro-front-end` · **Branch:** `main` · **Date:** 2026-05-04  
**Scope:** Full audit — dead code, duplicate pages, broken imports, repo hygiene, bundle, API surface, hook architecture, hardening gaps. Brenus excluded.

The frontend is a working React/Vite/MUI application with a well-structured modular route system and solid hook architecture. The core MOAT pipeline, Ayesha persona, and SL analyzer are all functional. However, the repo has accumulated significant technical debt across five categories: **repo hygiene** (Python backend code committed into the frontend), **duplicate pages** (two live AyeshaCompleteCare files), **dead/stale code** (6 .bak files, 30+ committed .md docs, 4 sl_patch directories), **runtime correctness gaps** (render.yaml rootDir mismatch, options merge bug, duplicate SL calls), and **bundle bloat** (manualChunks disabled, no code splitting). All findings are rated by severity and mapped to a prioritized fix list.

Additionally at repo root:
- `debug_raw_signals.py`
- `test_paclitaxel_payload.py`
- `verify_eligibility_adapter.py`
- `verify_workflow_store.py`
- `src/components/research/__init__.py`
- `src/pages/ResearchPortal/__init__.py`
**Note:** `.gitignore` already has `*.py[cod]` and `debug_*.py` but does NOT ignore `*.py` generally — so these files are tracked.

#### 1.4.2 useAyeshaCareData Options Merge Bug
**Severity: MEDIUM** — In `useAyeshaCareData`:
```js
const requestBody = buildRequest({ ...options, include_io_selection: true, max_trials: 50 });
generatePlan(profile, requestBody);
```
Then in `useCompleteCareOrchestrator.generatePlan`:
```js
const requestBody = { ...baseRequest, ...options };
```
The `options` parameter passed to `generatePlan` is the already-merged `requestBody` from `buildRequest`. This means the spread `{ ...baseRequest, ...options }` overwrites `baseRequest` with the merged object — which is correct. **However**, `buildRequest` in `useAyeshaProfile` sets `include_io_selection: false` by default. The `useAyeshaCareData` override sets it to `true`, but only if it survives the merge. This is fragile and depends on merge order.

#### 1.4.3 TrialReplaySandbox Uses Hardcoded localhost
**Severity: MEDIUM** — `src/components/ayesha/simulator/TrialReplaySandbox.jsx` line 11:
```js
const API_BASE_URL = import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL || 'http://localhost:8000';
```
This uses `VITE_API_URL` (not `VITE_API_ROOT`) and falls back to `localhost:8000`. In production, if `VITE_API_URL` is not set, all simulator API calls silently fail. All other components use `API_ROOT` from `src/lib/apiConfig.js`.

#### Fix 1.1 — Remove Python backend code from frontend repo
**Files to delete (git rm):**
```
src/components/orchestrator/Analysis/SyntheticLethality/sl_agent/   (entire dir)
src/components/orchestrator/Analysis/SyntheticLethality/sl_patch/    (entire dir)
src/components/orchestrator/Analysis/SyntheticLethality/sl_patch_rs/ (entire dir)
src/components/orchestrator/Analysis/SyntheticLethality/sl_patch_rs_2/ (entire dir)
src/components/research/__init__.py
src/pages/ResearchPortal/__init__.py
debug_raw_signals.py
test_paclitaxel_payload.py
verify_eligibility_adapter.py
verify_workflow_store.py
```
**Action:** `git rm -r` each path. Add `*.py` to `.gitignore` with exception for any intentional Python scripts (none currently needed in frontend).

slides.subtitle: Three assets. Three structural failures. One intelligence layer that sees all of them.

slides.title: The Problem Sanofi Has
slides.subtitle: Three assets. One shared failure mode: patient selection in MSS CRC.

columns.trial: NCT07500298 — Phase 1, 1L MSS mCRC, opens Sept 2026
columns.structural_flaw: No liver-met exclusion gate. CO.26 Pint=0.02 — IO is suppressed in 70–79% of enrolled patients.
CEACAM5 IHC alone is not enough. IO-suppressed (liver-met) MAS=0.597 vs IO-permissive MAS=0.741.

columns.trial: CARMEN-LC03 — failed ITT (PFS HR=1.14). EU clinical hold active.
columns.structural_flaw: Wrong gate: CEACAM5 ≥50% enrolled a harmful subgroup (50–79%, HR=1.38). ≥80% shows OS trend HR=0.71.

visual_directive.bottom_callout: MSS CRC is not IO-resistant by nature. It is IO-resistant by design failure. All three failures are fixable with the right patient selection gate.

slides.one_claim: All three Sanofi MSS CRC assets share the same root cause: the wrong patients were enrolled.

speaker_note.cue: Open cold. No pleasantries. Lead with the number they cannot argue with.
speaker_note.spoken: Your IO-activating asset — SAR445877 — is going to fail in liver-metastatic patients. Not because the drug doesn't work. Because liver metastases abolish IO benefit in MSS colorectal cancer. This is not a hypothesis. CO.26: durvalumab plus tremelimumab in refractory MSS CRC. Liver-met patients: PFS hazard ratio 1.39 — numerically harmful. Non-liver-met patients: PFS hazard ratio 0.54. Interaction p-value 0.02. AtezoTRIBE tested the same question with a VEGF-targeting agent. Liver-met interaction p-value: 0.990. No suppression. VEGF agents are not affected. IO-activating agents are. REGONIVO Ph
speaker_note.delivery: Pause after 'Same answer every time.' Let it land. Do not rush the Pint=0.02 — say it slowly. The audience will know what an interaction p-value means.

CARMEN-LC03 ITT PFS HR=1.14 — primary endpoint not met
SAR445953 IO-suppressed MAS=0.597 vs IO-permissive MAS=0.741
data_anchors.source: CrisPRO MAS scoring — direction confirmed by published HRs

hard_stops: Do not say 'SAR445877 will fail' — say 'liver-met suppression pattern consistent across three independent trials'
Do not disclose MAS formula or proprietary formula mechanics
MAS scores: label as 'CrisPRO-computed, direction confirmed by published HRs'
slides.transition_to_next: The first failure mode is liver mets. Slide 03 shows the data across three independent trials.

slides.subtitle: Liver metastases abolish IO benefit in MSS CRC — confirmed across three independent trials.

any claim that SAR445877 will definitely fail

slides.one_claim: IO suppression by liver mets is a reproducible biological phenomenon, not a trial-specific artifact.

speaker_note.cue: Open cold. No pleasantries. Lead with the number they cannot argue with.
speaker_note.spoken: Your IO-activating asset — SAR445877 — is going to fail in liver-metastatic patients. Not because the drug doesn't work. Because liver metastases abolish IO benefit in MSS colorectal cancer. This is not a hypothesis. CO.26: durvalumab plus tremelimumab in refractory MSS CRC. Liver-met patients: PFS hazard ratio 1.39 — numerically harmful. Non-liver-met patients: PFS hazard ratio 0.54. Interaction p-value 0.02. AtezoTRIBE tested the same question with a VEGF-targeting agent. Liver-met interaction p-value: 0.990. No suppression. VEGF agents are not affected. IO-activating agents are. REGONIVO Phase 2 in the United States: five responders out of seventy patients. All five had no liver metastases. Zero responses in liver-metastatic patients. Three independent trials. Three different mechanisms. Same answer every time. SAR445877 is an IO-activating PD-1 × IL-15 fusion. Liver metastases eliminate anti-tumor T cells via intrahepatic macrophage-mediated apoptosis. You are selecting patients on CEACAM5 expression. You are not screening out the patients where your drug is mechanistically blocked. That is the structural problem we are here to solve.
speaker_note.delivery: Pause after 'Same answer every time.' Let it land. Do not rush the Pint=0.02 — say it slowly. The audience will know what an interaction p-value means.
speaker_note.key_pause: Pause after 'Same answer every time.' Let it land. Do not rush the Pint=0.02.

data_anchors.claim: AtezoTRIBE liver-met Pint PFS=0.990 — no suppression (VEGF contrast)
REGONIVO: 0/5 liver-met responders; all 5 responders had no liver mets

hard_stops: AtezoTRIBE Pint=0.990 means NO suppression — it is the VEGF contrast proving IO-specific suppression, not another failure
Do not say SAR445877 will fail — say 'liver-met suppression pattern consistent across three independent trials'
Do not conflate REGONIVO mechanism with SAR445877 — both are IO-activating but different classes
slides.transition_to_next: Liver mets explain why IO fails in 70–79% of patients. Slide 04 shows how to find the 10–15% who respond.

slides.title: Signal 2: TMB-DECODE
slides.subtitle: Tissue TMB fails. Plasma TMB works. The two assays measure different things.

left_column.heading: Tissue TMB ≥10 — NOT predictive
left_column.visual: HR bar in grey — HR=0.71, wide CI crossing 1.0
left_column.label: Standard assay — wrong modality

right_column.heading: Plasma TMB ≥28 — PREDICTIVE
right_column.visual: HR bar in green — HR=0.34, tight CI below 1.0
right_column.label: 66% reduction in hazard of death

connector_stat.interpretation: They are measuring different biological constructs

visual_directive.bottom_callout: pTMB captures circulating tumor DNA from ALL active lesions — including liver mets. Tissue TMB captures one biopsy site. In liver-met-dominant disease, tissue TMB is the wrong assay.
do_not_show: pTMB as a validated biomarker — it is exploratory

slides.one_claim: The patients who benefit from IO in MSS CRC are identifiable — they have high plasma TMB, not high tissue TMB.

speaker_note.cue: Pivot from problem to precision tool. This is where you show them the key.
speaker_note.spoken: Now here is what you are missing in your current trial design. CO.26 also ran a biomarker analysis. Tissue TMB — the standard assay — was not predictive. Hazard ratio 0.71, p-value 0.47. Not significant. But plasma TMB at a threshold of 28 variants per megabase? Hazard ratio 0.34. 90 percent confidence interval 0.18 to 0.63. P-value 0.022. That is a 66 percent reduction in the hazard of death. In refractory MSS colorectal cancer. With a checkpoint inhibitor. The reason tissue TMB fails and plasma TMB works is not mysterious. Tissue TMB measures clonal mutations in archival tissue from one biopsy site. Plasma TMB measures circulating tumor DNA from all active lesions simultaneously — including subclonal mutations that generate neoantigens. The Spearman correlation between the two assays in CO.26 was 0.13. They are measuring different things. The patients who benefit from IO in MSS CRC are identifiable. They are approximately 10 to 15 percent of the MSS population. They have high circulating mutational burden — not high tissue TMB. Your current SAR445877 trial design does not pre-specify plasma TMB as a stratification variable. That means if the signal is there, you will not be powered to see it. We can tell you exactly which patients to stratify on, and why. The cost of adding a GuardantOMNI blood draw to your protocol is trivial. The cost of not adding it is a failed trial that buries a real signal.
speaker_note.delivery: Read the CI slowly: 'zero point one eight to zero point six three.' That range is tight enough to be credible. The 0.13 correlation is the intellectual kill shot — say it plainly and let the audience do the math.
speaker_note.key_pause: Read the CI slowly: 'zero point one eight to zero point six three.' The 0.13 correlation is the intellectual pivot — pause on it.

data_anchors.claim: Tissue TMB ≥10 OS HR=0.71 (p=0.47) — not predictive
data_anchors.source: PMID 38727700, Loree et al. Clin Cancer Res 2024 (SRC-02)

data_anchors.claim: pTMB vs tissue TMB Spearman r=0.13 (p=0.20)

hard_stops: pTMB ≥28 is EXPLORATORY — say 'strongest biomarker signal in the field', NOT 'validated biomarker'
Use 90% CI 0.18–0.63 only — not the old 95% CI (corrected in all dossier files)
Do not say NCT07500298 will fail without pTMB — say 'the signal will be diluted'
slides.transition_to_next: pTMB identifies the IO-permissive patients. Slide 05 shows what happens when you enroll the wrong CEACAM5 gate.

slides.title: Signal 3: GATE-FAIL
slides.subtitle: CARMEN-LC03 failed because of the wrong enrollment gate — not the wrong drug.

timeline.event: ITT PFS HR=1.14 — primary endpoint not met
EU clinical hold — France, Spain, Sweden, Netherlands

slides.one_claim: The CARMEN-LC03 failure is a gate failure, not a drug failure. The ≥80% subgroup carries the signal.

speaker_note.cue: Shift to the market opportunity. This is the BD argument.
speaker_note.spoken: Now let us talk about where your drug actually works. You have been developing SAR445877 and SAR445953 in NSCLC and in unselected CRC. The CARMEN-LC03 trial failed because 45 percent of enrolled patients were in a subgroup where tusamitamab ravtansine was numerically harmful — CEACAM5 50 to 79 percent, PFS hazard ratio 1.38. The 80 percent and above subgroup showed an OS trend of 0.71. The gate was wrong. The drug was not. Here is what the CRC literature tells you about the right gate. Jansen et al. 2024 in Cancers: pan-tumor tissue microarray, 13,725 samples, 120 tumor types. CEACAM5 positivity in colorectal adenocarcinomas: 98.7 percent. Near-universal. Warmerdam et al. 2025 in EJNMMI Rese
speaker_note.delivery: The 98.7% number is striking — pause after it. The MMR-deficiency finding (P<0.0001) is the scientific precision that separates this from a marketing claim. Say it with the p-value.
speaker_note.key_pause: The 98.7% number is striking — pause after it. The MMR-deficiency finding (P<0.0001) is the scientific precision that separates this from a marketing claim.

data_anchors.claim: CARMEN-LC03 ITT PFS HR=1.14 — primary endpoint not met
CEACAM5 ≥80% OS HR=0.71 — post-hoc trend (CI not published)
CRC CEACAM5 any-positivity 98.7%; MSS CRC has HIGHEST CEACAM5 (P<0.0001)

hard_stops: CEACAM5 ≥80% OS HR=0.71 is POST-HOC, UNDERPOWERED, CI NOT PUBLISHED — say 'directional trend', not 'established efficacy'
Do not say tusamitamab is a failed drug — say 'wrong gate, not wrong drug'
EU clinical hold is a fact (CR-27) — state it neutrally, do not editorialize
slides.transition_to_next: The ≥80% gate rescues the CEACAM5 signal. Slide 06 shows the liquid biopsy layer that makes it actionable.

slides.subtitle: Within CEACAM5 ≥80%, serum CEA identifies a 5× ORR enrichment — without a biopsy.

correlation_box.interpretation: Standard CEA blood test is sufficient for liquid biopsy pre-screen

speaker_note.cue: This is the BD argument. The science is done. Now show them the economics.
speaker_note.spoken: The two-gate system is not a hypothesis. It is derived from the same published data you just saw. Together they identify the patients most likely to respond — and reduce the cost of finding a responder by 5 to 12 times. The current unselected enrollment costs $312,000 to $714,000 per responder. The two-gate enrollment costs $56,000 to $143,000. That is not a rounding error. That is the difference between a trial that is economically viable and one that is not.

speaker_note.cue: Land the ask. Be specific. Give them a number, a timeline, and a decision.
speaker_note.spoken: Here is what we are proposing. One immediate action. One three-month study. One hundred and fifty thousand dollars. Commission a 50-patient retrospective IHC study on archival MSS CRC tissue using your own clone 769 antibody at your own eligibility threshold. Measure CEACAM5 80-percent-and-above prevalence in the MSS mCRC population. That single data point replaces the NSCLC proxy in every pool size calculation in your program. If the true CRC prevalence is 40 percent instead of 28 percent, your addressable pool grows from 13,090 to 18,700 patients per year in the US. If it is 50 percent, the pool is 23,375. That is a 43 to 79 percent expansion of your commercial opportunity from a three-month, $150,000 study on tissue you already have in your biobank. In parallel, we are proposing BreAK CRC-001 — a randomized Phase 2 trial of STC-1010 added to mFOLFOX6 plus or minus bevacizumab in first-line MSS metastatic CRC. The backbone is identical to your SAR445877 program. The biomarker infrastructure we are building — liver-met stratification, plasma TMB pre-specification, CEACAM5 gate validation in CRC — is directly transferable to your trial design. We are asking for three data points: the CARMEN-LC03 80-percent subgroup data with confidence intervals, the SAR445877 Phase 1 liver-met response data, and the clone 769 IHC study. In exchange, you get a validated biomarker framework for MSS CRC IO that no one in the field has built yet. You have the drug and the tissue. We have the analytical infrastructure. That is the partnership.
speaker_note.delivery: The $150K number is the anchor. Say it before the pool expansion math. The sequence is: small cost → large upside → here is what we need from you. End on 'That is the partnership.' Do not add anything after that sentence.
speaker_note.key_pause: After reading Ask 03, pause. Then say: 'One hundred and fifty thousand dollars. Three months. That is the decision on the table today.'

speaker_note.cue: Open cold. No pleasantries. Lead with the number they cannot argue with.
speaker_note.spoken: Your IO-activating asset — SAR445877 — is going to fail in liver-metastatic patients. Not because the drug doesn't work. Because liver metastases abolish IO benefit in MSS colorectal cancer. This is not a hypothesis. CO.26: durvalumab plus tremelimumab in refractory MSS CRC. Liver-met patients: PFS hazard ratio 1.39 — numerically harmful. Non-liver-met patients: PFS hazard ratio 0.54. Interaction p-value 0.02. AtezoTRIBE tested the same question with a VEGF-targeting agent. Liver-met interaction p-value: 0.990. No suppression. VEGF agents are not affected. IO-activating agents are. REGONIVO Phase 2 in the United States: five responders out of seventy patients. All five had no liver metastases. Zero responses in liver-metastatic patients. Three independent trials. Three different mechanisms. Same answer every time. SAR445877 is an IO-activating PD-1 × IL-15 fusion. Liver metastases eliminate anti-tumor T cells via intrahepatic macrophage-mediated apoptosis. You are selecting patients on CEACAM5 expression. You are not screening out the patients where your drug is mechanistically blocked. That is the structural problem we are here to solve.

raw.title: Signal 2: TMB-DECODE
raw.subtitle: Tissue TMB fails. Plasma TMB works. The two assays measure different things.

left_column.heading: Tissue TMB ≥10 — NOT predictive
left_column.visual: HR bar in grey — HR=0.71, wide CI crossing 1.0
left_column.label: Standard assay — wrong modality

right_column.heading: Plasma TMB ≥28 — PREDICTIVE
right_column.visual: HR bar in green — HR=0.34, tight CI below 1.0
right_column.label: 66% reduction in hazard of death

connector_stat.interpretation: They are measuring different biological constructs

visual_directive.bottom_callout: pTMB captures circulating tumor DNA from ALL active lesions — including liver mets. Tissue TMB captures one biopsy site. In liver-met-dominant disease, tissue TMB is the wrong assay.
do_not_show: pTMB as a validated biomarker — it is exploratory

raw.one_claim: The patients who benefit from IO in MSS CRC are identifiable — they have high plasma TMB, not high tissue TMB.

speaker_note.cue: Pivot from problem to precision tool. This is where you show them the key.
speaker_note.spoken: Now here is what you are missing in your current trial design. CO.26 also ran a biomarker analysis. Tissue TMB — the standard assay — was not predictive. Hazard ratio 0.71, p-value 0.47. Not significant. But plasma TMB at a threshold of 28 variants per megabase? Hazard ratio 0.34. 90 percent confidence interval 0.18 to 0.63. P-value 0.022. That is a 66 percent reduction in the hazard of death. In refractory MSS colorectal cancer. With a checkpoint inhibitor. The reason tissue TMB fails and plasma TMB works is not mysterious. Tissue TMB measures clonal mutations in archival tissue from one biopsy site. Plasma TMB measures circulating tumor DNA from all active lesions simultaneously — including subclonal mutations that generate neoantigens. The Spearman correlation between the two assays in CO.26 was 0.13. They are measuring different things. The patients who benefit from IO in MSS CRC are identifiable. They are approximately 10 to 15 percent of the MSS population. They have high circulating mutational burden — not high tissue TMB. Your current SAR445877 trial design does not pre-specify plasma TMB as a stratification variable. That means if the signal is there, you will not be powered to see it. We can tell you exactly which patients to stratify on, and why. The cost of adding a GuardantOMNI blood draw to your protocol is trivial. The cost of not adding it is a failed trial that buries a real signal.
speaker_note.delivery: Read the CI slowly: 'zero point one eight to zero point six three.' That range is tight enough to be credible. The 0.13 correlation is the intellectual kill shot — say it plainly and let the audience do the math.
speaker_note.key_pause: Read the CI slowly: 'zero point one eight to zero point six three.' The 0.13 correlation is the intellectual pivot — pause on it.

speaker_note.cue: Shift to the market opportunity. This is the BD argument.
speaker_note.spoken: Now let us talk about where your drug actually works. You have been developing SAR445877 and SAR445953 in NSCLC and in unselected CRC. The CARMEN-LC03 trial failed because 45 percent of enrolled patients were in a subgroup where tusamitamab ravtansine was numerically harmful — CEACAM5 50 to 79 percent, PFS hazard ratio 1.38. The 80 percent and above subgroup showed an OS trend of 0.71. The gate was wrong. The drug was not. Here is what the CRC literature tells you about the right gate. Jansen et al. 2024 in Cancers: pan-tumor tissue microarray, 13,725 samples, 120 tumor types. CEACAM5 positivity in colorectal adenocarcinomas: 98.7 percent. Near-universal. Warmerdam et al. 2025 in EJNMMI Rese

speaker_note.spoken: Here is what we are proposing. One immediate action. One three-month study. One hundred and fifty thousand dollars. If the true CRC prevalence is 40 percent instead of 28 percent, your addressable pool grows from 13,090 to 18,700 patients per year in the US. If it is 50 percent, the pool is 23,375. That is a 43 to 79 percent expansion of your commercial opportunity from a three-month, $150,000 study on tissue you already have in your biobank. In parallel, we are proposing BreAK CRC-001 — a randomized Phase 2 trial of STC-1010 added to mFOLFOX6 plus or minus bevacizumab in first-line MSS metastatic CRC. The backbone is identical to your SAR445877 program. The biomarker infrastructure we are building — liver-met stratification, plasma TMB pre-specification, CEACAM5 gate validation in CRC — is directly transferable to your trial design. We are asking for three data points: the CARMEN-LC03 80-percent subgroup data with confidence intervals, the SAR445877 Phase 1 liver-met response data, and the clone 769 IHC study. In exchange, you get a validated biomarker framework for MSS CRC IO that no one in the field has built yet. You have the drug and the tissue. We have the analytical infrastructure. That is the partnership.

attributes.preview:
# AACR Session Slug Navigation — Gap Analysis & Fix Plan

> **Status:** Documented gap (not yet implemented)  
> **Example session slug:** `hematological-malignancies-undruggable-targets-and-new-frontiers`  
> **Example talk UID:** `hematological-malignancies-undruggable-targets-and-new-frontiers::woyach::2`  
> **Related:** [AACR_SUPABASE_CONNECTION.md](./AACR_SUPABASE_CONNECTION.md), [INTEL_FACETED_FILTERING.md](./INTEL_FACETED_FILTERING.md)

---

## What the user expects

Click a session identifier (e.g. `hematological-malignancies-undruggable-targets-and-new-frontiers`) and see:

- Session title and metadata
- All speakers / talks in that session
- Links to each lead, Schema A talk, and Schema B intel record

**This does not exist today.**

---

## What exists today (verified — not assumed)

### Talk UID format

Canonical talk IDs are three-part strings:

```
{session_slug}::{speaker_slug}::{index}
```

Example:

```
hematological-malignancies-undruggable-targets-and-new-frontiers::woyach::2
│                                                                      │      │
└─ session_slug                                                        │      └─ talk index within session/speaker
                                                                       └─ speaker slug
```

Slug extraction is already implemented for crosswalk logic:

```python
def slug_from_talk_id(talk_id: str) -> str | None:
    if not talk_id:
        return None
    return talk_id.split("::", 1)[0] or None
```

Source: `scripts/aacr_supabase_load.py`

### Supabase session index

Per `docs/AACR_SUPABASE_CONNECTION.md`:

| Table | Rows | Role |
|-------|-----:|------|
| `aacr_sessions` | 287 | Session index (**slug as primary key**) |
| `aacr_session_metadata` | 287 | Full session JSON from `sessions.jsonl` |
| `aacr_speakers` | 862 | Schema A — includes `session_slug` column |
| `aacr_competitive_intel` | 926 | Schema B — `talk_id` embeds session slug as first segment |

Source data: `sessions.jsonl` / `sessions.csv` in [aacr-2026 repo](https://github.com/fjkiani/aacr-2026) → `data/master/`

### CRM frontend routes (no session route)

Current router (`crm-deployment/frontend/src/router.js`):

| Route | Page |
|-------|------|
| `/crm/leads` | Leads list |
| `/crm/leads/:leadId` | Lead detail (GTM Intel tab) |
| `/crm/competitive-intel` | Schema B corpus browser |

**Missing:** `/crm/sessions/:sessionSlug` or `/crm/aacr/sessions/:sessionSlug`

### Where session slug appears but is not

| Failure type | Evidence strength | Failing compound / target | Mechanistic blindspot | Ghost-responder admission |
|--------------|-------------------|---------------------------|----------------------|---------------------------|
| `primary_resistance` | `explicit_admission` | Covalent BTK inhibitors (ibrutinib, acalabrutinib, zanubrutinib) | Reliance on C481 cysteine binding; mutations at C481S, T474I, L528W prevent irreversible covalent attachment | some patients achieve long PFS but eventually progress due to mutations |

Plus: 5 CrisPRO opportunities, moat weaknesses, trial risks, competitors cited, watchlist signals.

---

## Root cause (verified in code — not assumed)

### 1. Two separate data layers, one UI stack

`TrackerIntel.vue` renders **three independent panels** stacked vertically:

```vue
<!-- Panel 1: CRM Lead doc fields only -->
<IntelPanel :schema="gtmSchema" :record="doc" />

<!-- Panel 2: Schema B — separate API call -->
<IntelPanel v-if="intel" :schema="aacrIntelSchema" :record="intel" />

<!-- Panel 3: Schema A — separate API call -->
<IntelPanel v-if="talk" :schema="aacr2026Schema" :record="talk" />
```

Source: `crm-deployment/frontend/src/components/TrackerIntel.vue`

- **GTM panel** reads `gtmSchema` fields directly off the CRM Lead `doc`.
- **Schema B panel** loads via `get_aacr_intel({ talk_id: doc.source_ref_id })`.
- There is **no code path** that writes Schema B content into G

attributes.preview:
# Intel Faceted Filtering — Architecture

> **Status:** Design spec (not yet implemented)  
> **Scope:** Dynamic, scalable Leads list filtering across GTM (CRM Lead), Schema A (AACR Talk), and Schema B (AACR Intel)  
> **Related:** [AACR_SUPABASE_CONNECTION.md](./AACR_SUPABASE_CONNECTION.md), [GTM_SCHEMA_B_BRIDGE.md](./GTM_SCHEMA_B_BRIDGE.md), [AACR_SESSION_NAVIGATION.md](./AACR_SESSION_NAVIGATION.md)

---

## Problem

The Leads list (`/crm/leads`) uses the stock Frappe CRM view stack:

```
Leads.vue → ViewControls → crm.api.doc.get_data → frappe.get_list("CRM Lead")
```

- Default columns are classic CRM only (name, org, status, email, mobile, assignee, modified).
- GTM fields (`tier`, `lead_score`, `source_ref_id`, narrative fields) exist on the lead but are not in default columns or quick filters.
- Schema B dimensions (opportunity count, vulnerabilities, presentation type) live in `AACR Intel` child tables and are **not joinable** through `get_data`.
- ~906 AACR leads appear as an undifferentiated dump because intel facets are invisible at list time.

Hard-coded saved views or one-off custom fields per intel dimension do not scale when Schema C/D are added.

---

## Design principle

Mirror the display pattern already in production:

| Layer | Display (exists today) | Filter (target) |
|-------|------------------------|-----------------|
| Config | `gtmSchema`, `aacrIntelSchema`, `aacr2026Schema` | Filter manifest derived from same schema family |
| Renderer | `IntelPanel` + type registry | `DynamicFilterBar` + filter type registry |
| Data | Per-lead API fetch at detail time | Materialized facet index at list time |

**Rule:** Schema defines both what you **show** and what you **filter** — not separate hard-coded filter lists in Vue.

---

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph sources [Intel sources]
    GTM[GTM fields on CRM Lead]
    SA[Schema A - AACR Talk]
    SB[Schema B - AACR Intel]
    Future[Future schemas...]
  end

  subgraph pipeline [Sync pipeline]
    Rebuild["rebuild_lead_facets(lead_id)"]
  end

  subgraph index [Queryable index]
    Facets["Lead Intel Facets\n1:1 with CRM Lead"]
  end

  subgraph api [API layer]
    Manifest["get_filter_manifest('CRM Lead')"]
    Query["get_faceted_list(doctype, filters, facet_filters)"]
  end

  subgraph ui [Frontend]
    Bar["DynamicFilterBar"]
    List[Leads list via ViewControls]
  end

  GTM --> Rebuild
  SA --> Rebuild
  SB --> Rebuild
  Future --> Rebuild
  Rebuild --> Facets
  Ma

attributes.preview:
# CRISPRO: THE COMPUTATIONAL CANCER CURE ROADMAP
## From SL Engine to Universal Vulnerability Atlas

**Classification**: RESEARCH USE ONLY — Not for Clinical Decision Making  
**Author**: Fahad Kiani | CrisPRO.ai  
**Version**: v2.0 — April 2026  
**Status**: ACTIVE EXECUTION — No mercy. No pauses. No vapor.

---

> "The goal is not to build a better search engine for cancer papers.  
> The goal is to computationally enumerate every exploitable vulnerability  
> in every cancer genome — and make that knowledge actionable at the tumor board."

---

## PART I: WHERE WE ARE

### What's Real (as of April 2026)

The engine is not a prototype. It is a production system with:

- **43/43 tests passing** on the full merge gate
- **8 CandidateAxis values** with gene-aware mechanism strings
- **VALIDATED tier** for MBD4/gemcitabine (PMID 35428381) — the only axis that deserves it
- **RS framework** computing patient-specific replication stress scores from somatic mutation profiles
- **Frozen receipts** for CDK2/CCNE1_AMP (d=-0.72), POLR2K/TP53_RS (d=-1.25), WTAP/TP53_RS (d=-1.08), CDT1/TP53_RS (d=-1.00)
- **Stub mode** — no 503s on cold start
- **RUO invariant** — enforced at every layer, tested, non-negotiable

### What's Missing (The Gaps That Matter)

| Gap | Impact | Sprint |
|-----|--------|--------|
| RS context not wired into HTTP endpoint | CDK2/CCNE1_AMP shows Mechanistic instead of Strong for CCNE1-amp patients | A |
| No patient-level SL report endpoint | Tumor board has to make multiple API calls | B |
| No lineage dependency engine | PAX8, PARD3 (ovarian) not surfaced | C |
| WRN/MSI-H receipts missing | MLH1/MSH2 queries return "Not supported" | D |
| BRCA/FA audit queue not wired | FANCM, FANCC, H2AFX lost | E |
| PDAC/TNBC receipts missing | BRCA2/olaparib (POLO trial) not in engine | F |
| Prostate/AML receipts missing | CDK12/IO, IDH1/PARP not in engine | G |
| No universal SL report endpoint | The endgame doesn't exist yet | H |

---

## PART II: THE COMPUTATIONAL CANCER CURE THESIS

### The Core Insight

Cancer is not one disease. It is a collection of genomic states, each with a unique vulnerability profile. The computational cure is not a single drug — it is a **complete enumeration of exploitable vulnerabilities** for every genomic state, delivered to the clinician at the moment of decision.

The thesis has three pillars:

**Pillar 1: Synthetic Lethality is the mechanism.**  
Every cancer genome has already acquired its first hit. The tumor's own mutations define its vulnerabilities. We don't need to find new drugs — we need to match existing drugs to the right genomic contexts. The SL engine does this.

**Pillar 2: Multi-modal evidence is the standard.**  
A CRISPR hit alone is not enough. A clinical receipt alone is not enough. The convergence of CRISPR + pharmacologic + in vitro + in vivo + clinical evidence is what makes a recommendation trustworthy. The multimodal fuser enforces this.

**Pillar 3: The pan-cancer atlas is the endg

attributes.preview:
# DepMap 24Q4 Synthetic Lethal Map — Resistance-State Analysis

## IMPORTANT DISCLAIMERS
- **ALL results are tagged: `HYPOTHESIS_TARGET_PENDING_PHASE1`**
- This is a **cell-line-based computational analysis only**
- Results are **NOT validated** in clinical data
- Do **NOT** claim clinical actionability from these findings
- SL interactions require experimental validation before clinical translation

---

## CRITICAL REVIEW FLAGS — READ BEFORE USING ANY RESULTS

Post-analysis scientific review identified the following issues. They affect interpretation of specific markers and must be disclosed in any downstream use.

| Marker | Severity | Issue |
|--------|----------|-------|
| **BCL2** | **SEVERE** | **Lineage confound**: BCL2 Q75 group is 37% lymphoid/myeloid (72 lymphoid + 29 myeloid / 276 lines) vs solid-tumor-dominated Q25 (Lung, Head/Neck, Bowel, Ovary). The 222 BCL2 hits likely reflect hematologic lineage essentialities, not BCL2-driven resistance biology in solid tumors. BCL2 is a lineage marker for lymphoid cells in DepMap, not a resistance marker. **BCL2 hits must not be used without lineage-corrected reanalysis restricted to solid tumor lines.** |
| **ZEB1** | **MODERATE** | **Lineage confound**: Q75 enriched for CNS/Brain (44/276), Peripheral Nervous System (32/276), Soft Tissue (32/276). Mesenchymal/neural lineage bias may inflate hit count. CHMP4B delta of -0.741 is numerically real but may reflect CNS/PNS-specific essentiality rather than ZEB1-driven EMT resistance. |
| **HIF1A** | **MODERATE** | **Expression range caveat**: HIF1A IQR = 1.64 log2 TPM+1; 84.7% of lines express above 5.0. Q75 vs Q25 split (7.03 vs 5.39) compares "moderately high" vs "moderate" expression — not high vs low HIF1A activity. HIF1A protein activity is regulated post-translationally by oxygen tension, absent in standard DepMap culture. VHL and EGLN1 appearing as hits confirms biologically coherent signal, but the narrow expression range limits confidence. |
| **ABCB1** | LOW | Tissue-specific expression; 30% of lines near-zero. Q25 threshold = 0.070 (near-zero). Sparse hits (n=7) may reflect tissue-specific biology. |
| **MMP9** | LOW | No major lineage confound identified. |
| **VEGFC** | LOW | No major lineage confound identified. High hit count (n=206) warrants lineage audit if used in downstream analysis. |

**Drug annotation failure (all markers):** DGIdb was rate-limited; OncoKB returned HTTP 401 (API key required). All 758 rows show `no_known_drug` in the `dr

# dbGaP Access Request — phs003428 (CALGB/SWOG 80405)
**CrisPRO EscapeMap Project | Immune Biomarker Validation for Bevacizumab Resistance in mCRC**

---

## 1. Study Summary (Confirmed from dbGaP Page)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **dbGaP Accession** | phs003428.v1.p1 |
| **Full Title** | CALGB/SWOG 80405: Genome-Wide Association Study of Patients with Advanced or Metastatic Colorectal Cancer Treated with First-Line Chemotherapy Combined with Cetuximab and/or Bevacizumab |
| **ClinicalTrials.gov** | NCT00265850 |
| **Principal Investigator** | Kouros Owzar, PhD |
| **Submitting Institution** | Duke University, Durham, North Carolina, USA |
| **Total Consented Subjects** | 1,464 |
| **Randomized Patients** | 2,334 (across 396 sites, Sept 2005 – March 2012) |
| **Study Design** | Randomized Phase III Clinical Trial; Multicenter |
| **Primary Phenotype** | Colorectal Neoplasms / Survival Analysis |
| **Access Portal** | https://dbgap.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/aa/wga.cgi?page=login (confirmed active) |
| **DUC Agreement** | https://dbgap.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/aa/wga.cgi?view_pdf&stacc=phs003428.v1.p1 |

**Study Background:** CALGB/SWOG 80405 randomized patients with untreated metastatic adenocarcinoma of the colon or rectum to bevacizumab, cetuximab, or the combination of both, in addition to FOLFOX or FOLFIRI chemotherapy. Germline DNA was prospectively collected and genome-wide association studies (GWAS) were conducted using clinical outcomes and molecular features. The combination arm was dropped in September 2009 due to lack of benefit and increased toxicity.

---

## 2. Data Types Available in phs003428 (Confirmed)

| Data Type | Confirmed | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Germline Genotype (GWAS)** | YES | ~700,000 SNPs from peripheral blood DNA; primary data type |
| **Phenotype / Clinical Outcomes** | YES | Includes OS, survival analysis variables |
| **Molecular Features (eQTL)** | YES | Expression quantitative trait loci analyses |
| **Clinical Trial Metadata** | YES | Treatment arm, chemotherapy regimen (FOLFOX/FOLFIRI), randomization data |
| **Tumor Molecular Markers** | YES | RAS/KRAS/NRAS mutations, BRAF mutations, MSI status |
| **RNA-seq Expression Data** | **NOT in phs003428** | RNA-seq (N=554 tumors) is in GEO as **GSE196576** (public, no access required) |

> **Critical Note:** The RNA-seq expression data and CIBERSORTx immune scores are already publicly available in **GSE196576** (GEO, public access). The dbGaP phs003428 dataset provides the **per-patient

raw.geo_title: Tumor immunogenomic features determine outcomes in metastatic colorectal cancer patients treated with standard-of-care combinations of bevacizumab and cetuximab
raw.geo_vs_dbgap_relationship: GSE196576 (GEO) contains PUBLICLY AVAILABLE processed RNA-seq expression data (N=469 primary tumors, 579 total samples) from the same CALGB/SWOG 80405 trial. The GEO data provides normalized gene expression values but DOES NOT include per-patient clinical outcome linkage (OS, PFS, treatment arm). The dbGaP controlled-access data (phs003428) contains the per-patient linked clinical outcomes (OS time, OS status, PFS time, PFS status, treatment arm assignment, and all clinical covariates) for N=1464 consented subjects. BOTH datasets are required for the M2-15 survival analysis: GEO for expression scores + dbGaP for per-patient OS/PFS/treatment linkage. The GEO data alone is insufficient for the proposed analysis.

SUBJECT: CTEP Pre-Notification — dbGaP Access Request for phs003428.v1.p1 (CALGB/SWOG 80405)

---

Dear CTEP Data Access Team,

This message serves as formal pre-notification, per CTEP Collaborative Agreement requirements, of an upcoming dbGaP Data Access Request (DAR) for the dataset phs003428.v1.p1 (CALGB/SWOG 80405), a study conducted under NCI/CTEP sponsorship with Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) as a collaborative partner. We respectfully request that BMS complete its standard 14-day review within the customary timeline following receipt of this notification.

**Study Requested**

Dataset: phs003428.v1.p1
Study Name: CALGB/SWOG 80405 — A Phase III Trial of FOLFOX/BEVACIZUMAB vs. FOLFOX/CETUXIMAB vs. FOLFOX/BEVACIZUMAB/CETUXIMAB for Patients with Untreated Metastatic Colorectal Cancer
Consent Group: DS-CA-COL (Disease-Specific: Cancer, COL)
Data Requested: All available RNA-seq expression data and linked clinical outcome variables (including: dead, fu_time, pf_time, pf_stat, TREAT_ASSIGNED)

**Principal Investigator**

Name: Fahad Kiani, PhD
Institution: CrisPRO / Brenus Pharma
Address: [FAHAD_INSERT: full institutional mailing address]
eRA Commons ID: [FAHAD_INSERT: eRA Commons username]

**Research Use Statement**

This project seeks to validate a 15-gene M2 macrophage transcriptomic panel (M2-15) as a bevacizumab-specific resistance predictor in metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC). Using RNA-seq expression data and clinical outcome variables from phs003428.v1.p1, we will compute M2-15 composite scores for each patient and apply multivariable Cox proportional hazards regression to assess the association between M2-15 scores and overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS), stratified by treatment arm (bevacizumab, cetuximab, and combination). This work directly extends the findings of Innocenti et al. 2022 (PMID 35176136), which identified M2 macrophage gene expression signatures as prognostically relevant in CALGB/SWOG 80405, by prospectively testing a refined, clinically actionable panel in a treatment-arm-specific framework. Full scientific justification for data access

This project seeks to validate a 15-gene M2 macrophage transcriptomic panel (M2-15) as a bevacizumab-specific resistance predictor in metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC). Using RNA-seq expression data and clinical outcome variables from phs003428.v1.p1, we will compute M2-15 composite scores for each patient and apply multivariable Cox proportional hazards regression to assess the association between M2-15 scores and overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS), stratified by treatment arm (bevacizumab, cetuximab, and combination). This work directly extends Innocenti et al. 2022 (PMID 35176136), which identified M2 macrophage gene expression signatures as prognostically relevant in CALGB/SWOG 80405, by prospectively testing a refined, clinically actionable panel in a treatment-arm-specific framework. The public GEO dataset GSE196576 provides the expression layer already accessible; the controlled-access phs003428.v1.p1 data are required for linkage to individual-level clinical outcomes and treatment assignment. No new patient contact will occur. All analyses will be conducted in a secure, access-controlled computing environment compliant with NIH dbGaP data security requirements.
Plain-language description of the research (~100 words)

Colorectal cancer is one of the most common cancers worldwide, and choosing the right targeted therapy remains a major clinical challenge. This study examines whether a specific pattern of immune cell activity — measured by 15 genes active in a type of immune cell called M2 macrophages — can predict which patients with advanced colorectal cancer will not respond well to bevacizumab, a widely used anti-cancer drug. We will analyze existing tumor gene expression data and treatment outcome records from the CALGB/SWOG 80405 clinical trial. No patients will be contacted, no new samples will be collected, and all data will be handled in a secure research environment. Results may help doctors select better treatments for individual patients in the future.

raw.action: NCI Data Access Committee (ncidac@mail.nih.gov) reviews the countersigned DAR for scientific merit, data use consistency with DS-CA-COL consent, and security compliance

raw.action: Upon DAC approval, execute Data Use Agreement (DUA) — Institutional SO signs the DUA on behalf of CrisPRO / Brenus Pharma; return signed DUA to NCI DAC
raw.notes: DUA template available at DUC PDF URL. SO must sign. Range: 7-14 days for institutional legal/compliance review and signature. Blocks S12.

raw.action: Download controlled-access data from phs003428.v1.p1 via dbGaP using SRA Toolkit or dbGaP file download interface; verify data integrity with provided checksums
raw.notes: days_to_clear=0 — download begins same day DUA is executed. Use SRA Toolkit (prefetch + fasterq-dump) or dbGaP download manager. Store in secure environment per data security plan. GEO GSE196576 (public expression layer) already accessible without this step.

raw.study_title: CALGB/SWOG 80405: Genome-Wide Association Study of Patients with Advanced or Metastatic Colorectal Cancer Treated with First-Line Chemotherapy Combined with Cetuximab and/or Bevacizumab

principal_investigator.name: Kouros Owzar
principal_investigator.institution: Duke University, Durham, North Carolina, USA

study_design.description: CALGB/SWOG 80405 (NCT00265850) was a randomized phase III study of bevacizumab, cetuximab, or the combination of these two monoclonal antibodies, in addition to systemic chemotherapy for patients with untreated metastatic adenocarcinoma of the colon or rectum. We prospectively collected germline DNA and conducted genome-wide association studies using clinical outcomes and molecular features.

datasets.molecular_data: Germline DNA genotyping (GWAS); RNA-seq data available via linked GEO accession GSE196576

linked_publications.note: Primary JAMA paper - main clinical outcomes
Innocenti et al. 2022 - Tumor immunogenomic features (M2 macrophage analysis)

geo_linked.title: Tumor immunogenomic features determine outcomes in metastatic colorectal cancer patients treated with standard-of-care combinations of bevacizumab and cetuximab
geo_linked.experiment_type: Expression profiling by high throughput sequencing
geo_linked.relationship_to_dbgap: GSE196576 contains the RNA-seq processed expression data (N=469-579 tumors) from the same CALGB/SWOG 80405 trial. The GEO data is PUBLICLY AVAILABLE and contains normalized expression values. The dbGaP controlled-access data (phs003428) contains the per-patient linked clinical outcomes (OS, PFS, treatment arm) and germline GWAS data. To link RNA-seq expression scores to individual patient OS/PFS outcomes, BOTH datasets are needed: GEO for expression + dbGaP for per-patient clinical linkage.
