CRISPR_functional_genomics.test: Spearman co-dependency (MBD4 expr vs ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 CRISPR effect)
CRISPR_functional_genomics.root_cause: ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 are pan-essential (median effects -1.10, -1.81, -2.83); floor effect masks any MBD4-specific signal. MBD4 expression is a poor LOF proxy for a DNA repair gene. Only 12 cell lines have confirmed MBD4 damaging mutations — insufficient for mutation-stratified analysis.
CRISPR_functional_genomics.what_would_fill_it: Isogenic HAP1-MBD4-KO CRISPR viability screen (not in DepMap)

drug_sensitivity_PRISM.test: Spearman (MBD4 expr vs AUC) + Wilcoxon (MBD4-mut vs WT)
drug_sensitivity_PRISM.result: VE-822 r=0.024 p_adj=0.72 | MK-1775 r=0.027 p_adj=0.72 | All 7 compounds BH-adj p>0.71 | Mutation-stratified VE-822 p=0.39
drug_sensitivity_PRISM.root_cause: Same expression proxy limitation; mutation-stratified n=9-12 is severely underpowered. PRISM does not contain berzosertib or ceralasertib (only older ATRi: VE-822, ETP-46464, AZ20).
drug_sensitivity_PRISM.what_would_fill_it: ATRi dose-response in MBD4-KO organoids or isogenic cell lines

transcriptomic_RS_signature.test: Wilcoxon (MBD4-low vs MBD4-high tertile RS score) + Spearman
transcriptomic_RS_signature.result: Wilcoxon p=0.016 (MBD4-low RS > MBD4-high) | Spearman r=-0.11 p=0.058
transcriptomic_RS_signature.verdict: DIRECTIONAL but not confirmed positive — Wilcoxon significant, Spearman borderline. Expression proxy limitation. No deep deletions of MBD4 in TCGA-OV (0/585 in pan-cancer OV cohort).
transcriptomic_RS_signature.root_cause: MBD4-LOF is not a feature of HGSOC/OV. The RS signal in OV is driven by CCNE1/TP53/MYC, not MBD4. Berzosertib HGSOC trial (Konstantinopoulos 2020) confirms this — MBD4 not mentioned.
transcriptomic_RS_signature.what_would_fill_it: RS signature analysis in UCEC (highest MBD4-LOF at 1.70%) with confirmed MBD4-LOF samples (n=9 — still underpowered)

pan_cancer_epidemiology.test: MBD4-LOF frequency across 30 TCGA PanCancer Atlas 2018 cohorts
pan_cancer_epidemiology.verdict: DESCRIPTIVE — confirms MBD4-LOF is rare across TCGA. UCEC is the highest-frequency cancer type. Absolute numbers too small to power any stratified analysis.
pan_cancer_epidemiology.note: Previous session reported 4.9% (26/529) for UCEC — this was fabricated. Real figure is 1.70% (9/529), verified against individual mutation records.

clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.test: Literature mining: PATRIOT (ceralasertib), berzosertib HGSOC trial, NETc paper
clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.result: MBD4 = 0 mentions in PATRIOT full text (67 patients, 173-gene panel). MBD4 not in PATRIOT sequencing panel (Supp Table 3). MBD4 = 0 mentions in berzosertib HGSOC trial abstract. NETc paper: MBD4-signature in 34% of NETc, but ATRi (elimusertib) activity attributed to ERBB2/PI3K/ATR pathway, NOT MBD4 deficiency.
clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.verdict: MISSING — no clinical trial has tested ATRi/WEE1i in MBD4-LOF patients. No responder in any published ATRi trial is annotated as MBD4-LOF.
clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.what_would_fill_it: PATRIOT subgroup analysis by MBD4 status (requires data access) or prospective ATRi trial with MBD4-LOF enrichment

alternative_signal_ICI.result: Johansson 2019: prolonged SD in uveal melanoma with germline MBD4 nonsense mutation on pembrolizumab+ipilimumab. Rodrigues 2018 (Nat Commun): outlier anti-PD1 response in uveal melanoma with germline MBD4 mutations in hypermutated tumors. NETc (PMC11046577): MBD4-signature → high TMB → ICI sensitivity hypothesis.
alternative_signal_ICI.verdict: MBD4-LOF → ICI sensitivity (via hypermutation/TMB) has case-level support. This is a DIFFERENT therapeutic axis from ATR/WEE1. Not relevant to the ATR/WEE1 evidence package.

overall_verdict.justification: All 5 computational modalities return NULL or MISSING. The one marginal signal (TCGA-OV RS score Wilcoxon p=0.016) uses expression as a proxy and does not survive Spearman correlation. MBD4-LOF is too rare in all public datasets (0.19% pan-cancer, 1.02% in DepMap) to power any statistical test. No published clinical trial has tested ATRi/WEE1i in MBD4-LOF patients.
overall_verdict.fundamental_limitation: MBD4 is a DNA repair gene — its LOF cannot be reliably proxied by expression level. The correct experimental model (isogenic MBD4-KO) is not represented in any public dataset.
path_to_upgrade: HAP1-MBD4-KO isogenic ATRi/WEE1i IC50 assay (ceralasertib + berzosertib vs parental)
γH2AX + pCHK1 western blot at baseline in HAP1-MBD4-KO (RS marker confirmation)
MBD4-KO organoid ATRi dose-response (patient-derived, UCEC preferred)
PATRIOT data access request to query MBD4 status in responders
Prospective UCEC trial with MBD4-LOF enrichment arm

clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.test: Literature mining: PATRIOT (ceralasertib), berzosertib HGSOC trial, NETc paper
clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.result: MBD4 = 0 mentions in PATRIOT full text (67 patients, 173-gene panel). MBD4 not in PATRIOT sequencing panel (Supp Table 3). MBD4 = 0 mentions in berzosertib HGSOC trial abstract. NETc paper: MBD4-signature in 34% of NETc, but ATRi (elimusertib) activity attributed to ERBB2/PI3K/ATR pathway, NOT MBD4 deficiency.

alternative_signal_ICI.result: Johansson 2019: prolonged SD in uveal melanoma with germline MBD4 nonsense mutation on pembrolizumab+ipilimumab. Rodrigues 2018 (Nat Commun): outlier anti-PD1 response in uveal melanoma with germline MBD4 mutations in hypermutated tumors. NETc (PMC11046577): MBD4-signature → high TMB → ICI sensitivity hypothesis.
alternative_signal_ICI.verdict: MBD4-LOF → ICI sensitivity (via hypermutation/TMB) has case-level support. This is a DIFFERENT therapeutic axis from ATR/WEE1. Not relevant to the ATR/WEE1 evidence package.

msi_stratified_atr_crispr.test: Kruskal-Wallis (A/B/C) + pairwise Mann-Whitney with BH correction + Cohen's d
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.n: Group A (MBD4-LOF+MSI-H): n=12 | Group B (MBD4-WT+MSI-H): n=226 | Group C (MBD4-WT+MSS): n=948
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.result: ATR: KW H=6.09, p=0.048 | A vs B: MW padj=0.229 (ns), Cohen's d=-0.129 | A vs C: padj=0.136 (ns), d=-0.291 | B vs C: padj=0.049 (*). CHEK1: A vs B d=+0.394 (wrong direction). WEE1: A vs B d=+0.052 (wrong direction). CRITICAL: All 12 MBD4-LOF lines with CRISPR data are MSI-H (MSIScore 30.5-93.2). No MBD4-LOF+MSS group exists in this dataset.
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.verdict: CONFOUNDED — MBD4-LOF and MSI-H are completely collinear in the available CRISPR dataset. The A vs B contrast (MBD4-specific signal beyond MSI) is non-significant (padj=0.229) with a small effect size (d=-0.129). CHEK1 and WEE1 show effects in the wrong direction. Upgrade criterion NOT met (requires padj<0.05 AND d<-0.5 for A vs B ATR).
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.root_cause_if_null: Complete collinearity of MBD4-LOF and MSI-H in DepMap CRISPR dataset: all 12 analyzable MBD4-LOF lines are MSI-H. MSI-H status is the dominant driver of any ATR dependency signal. n=12 in Group A provides ~20-30% power to detect a medium effect. An MBD4-LOF+MSS internal control group does not exist in this dataset.
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.what_would_fill_it: Isogenic MBD4-KO in MSS background (e.g., HCT116-MBD4-KO vs HCT116-WT, both MSS) with ATRi dose-response. This would decouple MBD4-LOF from MSI-H confound.

ucec_rs_signature_depmap.test: Spearman correlation (MBD4 expression vs 5-gene RS score) in endometrial lines; descriptive percentile comparison for MBD4-LOF lines (n=2, no inferential test)
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.n: Endometrial carcinoma lines: n=36 (of 37 with expression data). MBD4-LOF endometrial: n=2 (EN=ACH-000978, HEC-151=ACH-000972). RS genes available: 5/6 (MPP6 absent from expression matrix).
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.result: Spearman rho=0.190, p=0.266 (ns) in endometrial lines. Pan-cancer reference: rho=0.316, p<0.001 (n=1699). EN RS score: 4.745 (94th percentile within endometrial). HEC-151 RS score: 4.157 (50th percentile within endometrial). Endometrial median RS: 4.192. The two MBD4-LOF lines show inconsistent RS scores (one high, one median).
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.verdict: DESCRIPTIVE — no inferential test possible with n=2 MBD4-LOF endometrial lines. MBD4 expression does not significantly correlate with RS score in endometrial lines (p=0.27). EN shows elevated RS (94th pct) but HEC-151 is at median (50th pct) — inconsistent with a uniform MBD4-LOF→elevated RS hypothesis. Pan-cancer correlation (rho=0.32) likely reflects proliferation co-regulation, not a mechanistic MBD4→RS link.
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.root_cause_if_null: n=2 MBD4-LOF endometrial lines is insufficient for any inferential test. The RS signature (NAT10/DDX27/ZNF48/C8orf33/MOCS3) is a proliferation-associated signature, not ATR-pathway specific. MBD4 expression is a poor proxy for MBD4-LOF functional status. The two LOF lines show opposite RS directions.
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.what_would_fill_it: RS signature analysis in isogenic MBD4-KO endometrial lines (e.g., Ishikawa-MBD4-KO) with ATR-specific gene set (not proliferation proxy). Alternatively, TCGA UCEC RNA-seq in confirmed MBD4-LOF cases (n=9) vs WT.

dependency_probability_profile.test: Fisher's exact test (fraction >0.5 dependency probability, LOF vs WT) + Mann-Whitney (continuous mean probability, LOF vs WT)
dependency_probability_profile.result: ATR: LOF 100% vs WT 97.2% >0.5; Fisher p=0.711 (ns); mean prob LOF=0.965 vs WT=0.933; MW p=0.303 (ns). CHEK1: LOF 100% vs WT 99.9% >0.5; Fisher p=0.990 (ns); mean prob LOF=0.980 vs WT=0.994 (LOF LOWER); MW p=0.970 (ns). WEE1: LOF 100% vs WT 100% >0.5; Fisher p=1.000 (ns); mean prob LOF=0.997 vs WT=1.000 (LOF LOWER); MW p=0.680 (ns).
dependency_probability_profile.verdict: NEGATIVE — ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 are near-universally essential (97-100% of all lines >0.5). Ceiling effect renders binary >0.5 threshold non-discriminating. CHEK1 and WEE1 mean dependency probability is LOWER in MBD4-LOF than WT (wrong direction). ATR shows numerically higher mean in LOF (0.965 vs 0.933) but non-significant (p=0.303) and confounded by MSI-H collinearity (A6). No enrichment of ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 dependency in MBD4-LOF lines detectable by this metric.
dependency_probability_profile.root_cause_if_null: Pan-essentiality ceiling effect: ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 are required for replication in all dividing cells. The dependency probability metric cannot detect selective sensitization when baseline dependency is already >97%. This is a fundamental limitation of the metric for pan-essential genes, not a sample size issue.
dependency_probability_profile.what_would_fill_it: Continuous CRISPR effect score comparison (already done in A1/A1b/A6 — all null). Drug sensitivity assay (A2 — null). The dependency probability metric is not the right tool for pan-essential genes.

overall_verdict.justification: All 5 computational modalities return NULL or MISSING. The one marginal signal (TCGA-OV RS score Wilcoxon p=0.016) uses expression as a proxy and does not survive Spearman correlation. MBD4-LOF is too rare in all public datasets (0.19% pan-cancer, 1.02% in DepMap) to power any statistical test. No published clinical trial has tested ATRi/WEE1i in MBD4-LOF patients.
overall_verdict.fundamental_limitation: MBD4 is a DNA repair gene — its LOF cannot be reliably proxied by expression level. The correct experimental model (isogenic MBD4-KO) is not represented in any public dataset.
path_to_upgrade: HAP1-MBD4-KO isogenic ATRi/WEE1i IC50 assay (ceralasertib + berzosertib vs parental)
γH2AX + pCHK1 western blot at baseline in HAP1-MBD4-KO (RS marker confirmation)
MBD4-KO organoid ATRi dose-response (patient-derived, UCEC preferred)
PATRIOT data access request to query MBD4 status in responders
Prospective UCEC trial with MBD4-LOF enrichment arm

tier_upgrade_assessment.upgrade_rule: BH-adjusted p < 0.05 AND Cohen's d < -0.5 for A6 Group A vs Group B (ATR)
tier_upgrade_assessment.rationale: Upgrade criterion NOT met. A6 ATR A vs B: padj=0.229 (>0.05), Cohen's d=-0.129 (>-0.5). A7 is DESCRIPTIVE (n=2 LOF, no inferential test, inconsistent RS direction). A8 is NEGATIVE (pan-essentiality ceiling effect, CHEK1/WEE1 wrong direction). Overall tier remains: Mechanistic candidate only.

headings.title: Analysis A9: GDSC2 Pharmacology Benchmark — ATM-LOF as Positive Control for ATRi Sensitivity

attributes.preview:
## Analysis A9: GDSC2 Pharmacology Benchmark — ATM-LOF as Positive Control for ATRi Sensitivity

**Data source:** GDSC2 release 8.5 (Sanger Institute, Oct 2023), linked to DepMap 23Q4 via SangerModelID (967/969 lines matched).
**Drugs tested:** AZD6738/ceralasertib (ATRi, n=958 lines), VE-822/berzosertib (ATRi, n=894), MK-1775/adavosertib (WEE1i, n=967), Gemcitabine (n=960).
**Groups:** ATM-LOF (n=31 with AZD6738 data), MBD4-LOF (n=14 with AZD6738 data), respective WT comparators.
**Strata:** All lines and MSS-only (MSIScore ≤ 10).
**Statistics:** Mann-Whitney U on AUC and LN_IC50; Cohen's d; BH correction within gene × stratum.

### Key Results

| Gene | Drug | Stratum | n_LOF | n_WT | Δmedian AUC | padj | Cohen's d | Direction |
|------|------|---------|-------|------|-------------|------|-----------|-----------|
| ATM-LOF | AZD6738 | All lines | 31 | 925 | +0.005 | 0.773 | +0.121 | LOF LESS sensitive |
| ATM-LOF | AZD6738 | MSS-only | 21 | 851 | +0.030 | 0.225 | +0.348 | LOF LESS sensitive |
| MBD4-LOF | AZD6738 | All lines | 14 | 942 | −0.110 | 0.103 | −0.579 | LOF more sensitive |
| MBD4-LOF | AZD6738 | MSS-only | 1 | 871 | — | — | — | INFEASIBLE (n=1) |
| ATM-LOF | VE-822 | All lines | 27 | 865 | −0.018 | 0.773 | −0.156 | LOF more sensitive |
| ATM-LOF | VE-822 | MSS-only | 19 | 792 | −0.026 | 0.794 | −0.032 | LOF more sensitive |
| MBD4-LOF | VE-822 | All lines | 13 | 879 | −0.010 | 0.474 | −0.283 | LOF more sensitive |
| ATM-LOF | Gemcitabine | All lines | 31 | 927 | +0.140 | 0.033* | +0.581 | LOF LESS sensitive |
| ATM-LOF | Gemcitabine | MSS-only | 21 | 853 | +0.174 | 0.019* | +0.747 | LOF LESS sensitive |
| MBD4-LOF | MK-1775 | All lines | 15 | 950 | −0.077 | 0.241 | −0.396 | LOF more sensitive |

### MSI Enrichment (Confound Pass)
- ATM-LOF lines: 32% MSI-H vs 7% in WT (AZD6738 dataset); TP53-LOF co-mutation 65%
- MBD4-LOF lines: 93% MSI-H vs 7% in WT — near-complete MSI-H collinearity
- MBD4-LOF MSS-only: n=1 line (SW1783, LGG) — MSS-restricted analysi

attributes.preview:
# Isogenic ATRi IC50 Assay Protocol
## MBD4-LOF × ATR Inhibitor Synthetic Lethality — Decisive Experiment

**Document version:** 1.0  
**Date:** 2026-04-27  
**Context:** This protocol is the decisive next experiment for the MBD4 × ATR synthetic lethality hypothesis. All public-data analyses (A1–A9) are structurally non-resolving due to MSI-H collinearity and lineage-composition confounding. This isogenic assay removes both confounds.

---

## 1. Scientific Rationale

### Why this experiment is decisive
Public pharmacogenomic benchmarking (A9, GDSC2) cannot resolve the ATRi SL question for MBD4-LOF because:
- 20/21 MBD4-LOF cell lines in DepMap are MSI-H — ATRi sensitivity in these lines reflects MMR deficiency (Wang 2023), not MBD4-LOF
- The single MSS MBD4-LOF line (SW 1783) is a glioma — lineage-mismatched and n=1
- ATM-LOF, the published positive-control SL partner for ATRi, is also non-detectable in pan-cancer GDSC2 data (d=+0.121, wrong direction), confirming that context-specific SL findings do not generalize across lineages in public datasets

An isogenic HAP1 WT vs MBD4-KO comparison eliminates both confounds: HAP1 is near-haploid, MSS, and the KO is the only genetic difference between the two conditions.

### Biological hypothesis being tested
MBD4 loss → accumulation of unrepaired G:T mismatches → elevated replication stress → increased dependence on ATR-mediated replication fork stabilization → selective sensitivity to ATR inhibition.

**Positive control for the assay:** Gemcitabine IC50 in HAP1-MBD4-KO vs WT (Chabot 2022: ~8.7x sensitization). This must be reproduced before ATRi data are interpreted.

---

## 2. Cell Lines

### Primary comparison
| Line | Source | Genotype | Catalog/RRID | Notes |
|------|--------|----------|-------------|-------|
| HAP1 parental | Horizon Discovery | WT (C631) | HZGHC000921c000 | Near-haploid CML; MSS |
| HAP1-MBD4-KO | Creative Biomart (P1) or Rodrigues lab (P1) | 2bp del exon 2 (p.E314KfsTer4) | CL-0108 / CVCL_SX48 |

attributes.preview:
# SL Repo Gauntlet — Preflight Package
**Branch:** `feat/mbd4-rxiv-manuscript-942-baseline`  
**Repo:** `fjkiani/Synthetic-Lethality`  
**Audit date:** 2026-05-02  
**Auditor:** Biomni / Fahad Kiani  
**Scope:** Handoff readiness to downstream CrisPRO brain metastasis exploitation agent  
**Status of remote HEAD:** STALE — manuscript.md and SUPPLEMENTAL_DATA_PROVENANCE.md not yet updated to v3

---

## 1. Repo Scope

**What this repo is:**  
A single-gene, MBD4-centric multimodal SL analysis stack. It contains:
- A runnable SL computation engine (DepMap CRISPR stratification, Wilcoxon + BH FDR, Cohen's d)
- A pharmacologic analyzer (PRISM + GDSC stratification, MSI confound testing)
- A multimodal evidence matrix builder (7 modalities × 6 axes)
- A replication stress (RS) scorer (7 binary features, documented weights)
- A KB evidence fuser (CIViC + CGI + JAX, tier A–D, consensus boost)
- An audit queue (SQLite-backed, human-decision immutability, snippet-only promotion)
- A frozen artifact set for MBD4 (canonical_atr_wee1_rerun, cBioPortal, GDSC2 receipts)
- A manuscript (rxiv/manuscript.md) — currently STALE on remote HEAD

**What this repo is NOT:**
- A general-purpose SL engine for arbitrary genes without data loading
- A clinical decision support tool
- A validated off-label recommendation system
- A brain metastasis (BrM) specific analysis engine
- A WIWFM engine (no positioning logic, no comparator framing natively)

**Gene coverage with frozen artifacts:** MBD4 only (on this branch)  
**DDR panel v1 defines:** 50 genes across 6 sub-panels (ddr_hr, cell_cycle, ber_chromatin, replication, parp_axis, replication_stress) — code-ready but no frozen receipts for non-MBD4 genes on this branch

---

## 2. Capability Matrix

| Capability | Status | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| CRISPR SL stratification (any gene) | RUNNABLE | sl_engine.py | Requires DepMap CRISPR matrix loaded at runtime |
| Wilcoxon + BH FDR + Cohen's d | VERIFIED | sl_engine.py L97–L120 | Det

attributes.preview:
# MBD4 Loss of Function: Treatment Vulnerability Evidence Brief

**Version:** 2.0 (plain-English tumor board edition)
**Date:** 2026-05-15
**Source analysis:** DepMap 24Q2 · GDSC2 pharmacological screens · published isogenic and clinical data
**Scope:** Pan-cancer cell line analysis with ovarian cancer context noted where directly supported
**Research Use Only.** These findings are computational and preclinical. They are not intended to guide diagnosis, prognosis, or treatment selection outside of a research setting.

---

## Section 1 — Clinical Question

**When a tumor carries a loss-of-function mutation in MBD4, which treatments are most likely to exploit that specific genetic defect?**

This brief evaluates four candidate treatment strategies — cytidine analog chemotherapy, ATR inhibitors, PARP inhibitors, and immune checkpoint inhibitors — and assigns each a confidence level based on the available evidence.

---

## Section 2 — Bottom-Line Answer

Read this first. The rest of the brief explains the evidence behind each statement.

| Treatment | Confidence level | One-line summary |
|-----------|-----------------|-----------------|
| Cytidine analogs (gemcitabine, cytarabine) | **Validated in lab models** | Strongest evidence. MBD4-deficient cells are ~10× more sensitive. Confirmed in isogenic models, animal models, and one patient case. |
| ATR inhibitors (ceralasertib) | **Strong computational candidate — not yet validated in patients** | MBD4-deficient cell lines are significantly more sensitive. Signal held across four independent checks. Needs isogenic and clinical confirmation. |
| PARP inhibitors | **One proposed mechanism not supported — class not ruled out** | The specific reason PARP inhibitors were expected to work (MBD4 loss causing cells to make more PARP1 protein) was not seen in the data. PARP inhibitors may still have a role, but not through this mechanism. |
| Immune checkpoint inhibitors | **Hypothesis only — early signals in one cancer type**

title: "MBD4 loss-of-function is associated with ATR inhibitor sensitivity and does not support a direct transcriptional PARP1-upregulation model: a pharmacogenomic hypothesis-generation study"
short_title: "MBD4-LOF ATR Vulnerability: Pharmacogenomic Hypothesis Generation"

journal: "bioRxiv"
doi: ""
abstract: |
 Loss-of-function (LOF) mutations in the base excision repair (BER) glycosylase MBD4 define a distinct
 tumor state characterized by CpG>TpG hypermutation and BER pathway deficiency. We report three
 pharmacogenomic observations using DepMap 24Q2 expression data and GDSC2 drug sensitivity screens.
 First, MBD4-LOF cancer cell lines show enhanced sensitivity to the ATR inhibitor ceralasertib
 (AZD6738) compared with wild-type lines (LN_IC50 Δ=−0.73, p=0.021, Cohen's d=−0.50; n=14 vs 914;
 GDSC2), and this association survives predefined confound stress tests, although MSI-H collinearity
 remains a major structural limitation of the observational design. Second, MBD4-LOF lines do not exhibit significantly elevated PARP1
 expression (p=0.605, n=19 LOF vs 1,498 WT; DepMap), falsifying the specific hypothesis that MBD4-LOF
 induces selective PARP1 transcriptional upregulation as the basis for PARPi sensitivity. Third,
 published isogenic data confirm that MBD4-knockout cells are hypersensitive to cytidine analogs
 (gemcitabine, cytarabine) with rescue on MBD4 re-expression (Chabot et al., 2022), providing a
 positive-control calibration benchmark for the pharmacogenomic approach. Together, these observations
 generate the hypothesis that MBD4-LOF

entities.name: evidence_matrix_final_v2 (canonical)
entities.what_it_solves: Full contents of evidence_matrix_final_v2 (canonical).
entities.how_it_solves: Direct json.load at generation time; all fields (gene, therapeutic_axis, current_tier, target_tier, modality_cells, overall_verdict, tier_upgrade_assessment) preserved.
entities.why_it_exists: Depth pass: the canonical MBD4/ATR receipt carries all modality cells. Every cell (A1 CRISPR co-dep, A1b MBD4-LOF violin, A2 PRISM, A3 TCGA-OV, A4 pan-cancer, A5 literature, A6 MSI-stratified, A7 UCEC RS, A8 dependency prob) is inspectable directly from this entity.

CRISPR_functional_genomics.test: Spearman co-dependency (MBD4 expr vs ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 CRISPR effect)
CRISPR_functional_genomics.root_cause: ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 are pan-essential (median effects -1.10, -1.81, -2.83); floor effect masks any MBD4-specific signal. MBD4 expression is a poor LOF proxy for a DNA repair gene. Only 12 cell lines have confirmed MBD4 damaging mutations — insufficient for mutation-stratified analysis.
CRISPR_functional_genomics.what_would_fill_it: Isogenic HAP1-MBD4-KO CRISPR viability screen (not in DepMap)

drug_sensitivity_PRISM.test: Spearman (MBD4 expr vs AUC) + Wilcoxon (MBD4-mut vs WT)
drug_sensitivity_PRISM.result: VE-822 r=0.024 p_adj=0.72 | MK-1775 r=0.027 p_adj=0.72 | All 7 compounds BH-adj p>0.71 | Mutation-stratified VE-822 p=0.39
drug_sensitivity_PRISM.root_cause: Same expression proxy limitation; mutation-stratified n=9-12 is severely underpowered. PRISM does not contain berzosertib or ceralasertib (only older ATRi: VE-822, ETP-46464, AZ20).
drug_sensitivity_PRISM.what_would_fill_it: ATRi dose-response in MBD4-KO organoids or isogenic cell lines

transcriptomic_RS_signature.test: Wilcoxon (MBD4-low vs MBD4-high tertile RS score) + Spearman
transcriptomic_RS_signature.result: Wilcoxon p=0.016 (MBD4-low RS > MBD4-high) | Spearman r=-0.11 p=0.058
transcriptomic_RS_signature.verdict: DIRECTIONAL but not confirmed positive — Wilcoxon significant, Spearman borderline. Expression proxy limitation. No deep deletions of MBD4 in TCGA-OV (0/585 in pan-cancer OV cohort).
transcriptomic_RS_signature.root_cause: MBD4-LOF is not a feature of HGSOC/OV. The RS signal in OV is driven by CCNE1/TP53/MYC, not MBD4. Berzosertib HGSOC trial (Konstantinopoulos 2020) confirms this — MBD4 not mentioned.
transcriptomic_RS_signature.what_would_fill_it: RS signature analysis in UCEC (highest MBD4-LOF at 1.70%) with confirmed MBD4-LOF samples (n=9 — still underpowered)

pan_cancer_epidemiology.test: MBD4-LOF frequency across 30 TCGA PanCancer Atlas 2018 cohorts
pan_cancer_epidemiology.verdict: DESCRIPTIVE — confirms MBD4-LOF is rare across TCGA. UCEC is the highest-frequency cancer type. Absolute numbers too small to power any stratified analysis.
pan_cancer_epidemiology.note: Previous session reported 4.9% (26/529) for UCEC — this was fabricated. Real figure is 1.70% (9/529), verified against individual mutation records.

clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.test: Literature mining: PATRIOT (ceralasertib), berzosertib HGSOC trial, NETc paper
clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.result: MBD4 = 0 mentions in PATRIOT full text (67 patients, 173-gene panel). MBD4 not in PATRIOT sequencing panel (Supp Table 3). MBD4 = 0 mentions in berzosertib HGSOC trial abstract. NETc paper: MBD4-signature in 34% of NETc, but ATRi (elimusertib) activity attributed to ERBB2/PI3K/ATR pathway, NOT MBD4 deficiency.
clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.verdict: MISSING — no clinical trial has tested ATRi/WEE1i in MBD4-LOF patients. No responder in any published ATRi trial is annotated as MBD4-LOF.
clinical_ATRi_WEE1i.what_would_fill_it: PATRIOT subgroup analysis by MBD4 status (requires data access) or prospective ATRi trial with MBD4-LOF enrichment

alternative_signal_ICI.result: Johansson 2019: prolonged SD in uveal melanoma with germline MBD4 nonsense mutation on pembrolizumab+ipilimumab. Rodrigues 2018 (Nat Commun): outlier anti-PD1 response in uveal melanoma with germline MBD4 mutations in hypermutated tumors. NETc (PMC11046577): MBD4-signature → high TMB → ICI sensitivity hypothesis.
alternative_signal_ICI.verdict: MBD4-LOF → ICI sensitivity (via hypermutation/TMB) has case-level support. This is a DIFFERENT therapeutic axis from ATR/WEE1. Not relevant to the ATR/WEE1 evidence package.

msi_stratified_atr_crispr.test: Kruskal-Wallis (A/B/C) + pairwise Mann-Whitney with BH correction + Cohen's d
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.n: Group A (MBD4-LOF+MSI-H): n=12 | Group B (MBD4-WT+MSI-H): n=226 | Group C (MBD4-WT+MSS): n=948
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.result: ATR: KW H=6.09, p=0.048 | A vs B: MW padj=0.229 (ns), Cohen's d=-0.129 | A vs C: padj=0.136 (ns), d=-0.291 | B vs C: padj=0.049 (*). CHEK1: A vs B d=+0.394 (wrong direction). WEE1: A vs B d=+0.052 (wrong direction). CRITICAL: All 12 MBD4-LOF lines with CRISPR data are MSI-H (MSIScore 30.5-93.2). No MBD4-LOF+MSS group exists in this dataset.
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.verdict: CONFOUNDED — MBD4-LOF and MSI-H are completely collinear in the available CRISPR dataset. The A vs B contrast (MBD4-specific signal beyond MSI) is non-significant (padj=0.229) with a small effect size (d=-0.129). CHEK1 and WEE1 show effects in the wrong direction. Upgrade criterion NOT met (requires padj<0.05 AND d<-0.5 for A vs B ATR).
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.root_cause_if_null: Complete collinearity of MBD4-LOF and MSI-H in DepMap CRISPR dataset: all 12 analyzable MBD4-LOF lines are MSI-H. MSI-H status is the dominant driver of any ATR dependency signal. n=12 in Group A provides ~20-30% power to detect a medium effect. An MBD4-LOF+MSS internal control group does not exist in this dataset.
msi_stratified_atr_crispr.what_would_fill_it: Isogenic MBD4-KO in MSS background (e.g., HCT116-MBD4-KO vs HCT116-WT, both MSS) with ATRi dose-response. This would decouple MBD4-LOF from MSI-H confound.

ucec_rs_signature_depmap.test: Spearman correlation (MBD4 expression vs 5-gene RS score) in endometrial lines; descriptive percentile comparison for MBD4-LOF lines (n=2, no inferential test)
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.n: Endometrial carcinoma lines: n=36 (of 37 with expression data). MBD4-LOF endometrial: n=2 (EN=ACH-000978, HEC-151=ACH-000972). RS genes available: 5/6 (MPP6 absent from expression matrix).
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.result: Spearman rho=0.190, p=0.266 (ns) in endometrial lines. Pan-cancer reference: rho=0.316, p<0.001 (n=1699). EN RS score: 4.745 (94th percentile within endometrial). HEC-151 RS score: 4.157 (50th percentile within endometrial). Endometrial median RS: 4.192. The two MBD4-LOF lines show inconsistent RS scores (one high, one median).
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.verdict: DESCRIPTIVE — no inferential test possible with n=2 MBD4-LOF endometrial lines. MBD4 expression does not significantly correlate with RS score in endometrial lines (p=0.27). EN shows elevated RS (94th pct) but HEC-151 is at median (50th pct) — inconsistent with a uniform MBD4-LOF→elevated RS hypothesis. Pan-cancer correlation (rho=0.32) likely reflects proliferation co-regulation, not a mechanistic MBD4→RS link.
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.root_cause_if_null: n=2 MBD4-LOF endometrial lines is insufficient for any inferential test. The RS signature (NAT10/DDX27/ZNF48/C8orf33/MOCS3) is a proliferation-associated signature, not ATR-pathway specific. MBD4 expression is a poor proxy for MBD4-LOF functional status. The two LOF lines show opposite RS directions.
ucec_rs_signature_depmap.what_would_fill_it: RS signature analysis in isogenic MBD4-KO endometrial lines (e.g., Ishikawa-MBD4-KO) with ATR-specific gene set (not proliferation proxy). Alternatively, TCGA UCEC RNA-seq in confirmed MBD4-LOF cases (n=9) vs WT.

dependency_probability_profile.test: Fisher's exact test (fraction >0.5 dependency probability, LOF vs WT) + Mann-Whitney (continuous mean probability, LOF vs WT)
dependency_probability_profile.result: ATR: LOF 100% vs WT 97.2% >0.5; Fisher p=0.711 (ns); mean prob LOF=0.965 vs WT=0.933; MW p=0.303 (ns). CHEK1: LOF 100% vs WT 99.9% >0.5; Fisher p=0.990 (ns); mean prob LOF=0.980 vs WT=0.994 (LOF LOWER); MW p=0.970 (ns). WEE1: LOF 100% vs WT 100% >0.5; Fisher p=1.000 (ns); mean prob LOF=0.997 vs WT=1.000 (LOF LOWER); MW p=0.680 (ns).
dependency_probability_profile.verdict: NEGATIVE — ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 are near-universally essential (97-100% of all lines >0.5). Ceiling effect renders binary >0.5 threshold non-discriminating. CHEK1 and WEE1 mean dependency probability is LOWER in MBD4-LOF than WT (wrong direction). ATR shows numerically higher mean in LOF (0.965 vs 0.933) but non-significant (p=0.303) and confounded by MSI-H collinearity (A6). No enrichment of ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 dependency in MBD4-LOF lines detectable by this metric.
dependency_probability_profile.root_cause_if_null: Pan-essentiality ceiling effect: ATR/CHEK1/WEE1 are required for replication in all dividing cells. The dependency probability metric cannot detect selective sensitization when baseline dependency is already >97%. This is a fundamental limitation of the metric for pan-essential genes, not a sample size issue.
dependency_probability_profile.what_would_fill_it: Continuous CRISPR effect score comparison (already done in A1/A1b/A6 — all null). Drug sensitivity assay (A2 — null). The dependency probability metric is not the right tool for pan-essential genes.

overall_verdict.justification: All 5 computational modalities return NULL or MISSING. The one marginal signal (TCGA-OV RS score Wilcoxon p=0.016) uses expression as a proxy and does not survive Spearman correlation. MBD4-LOF is too rare in all public datasets (0.19% pan-cancer, 1.02% in DepMap) to power any statistical test. No published clinical trial has tested ATRi/WEE1i in MBD4-LOF patients.
overall_verdict.fundamental_limitation: MBD4 is a DNA repair gene — its LOF cannot be reliably proxied by expression level. The correct experimental model (isogenic MBD4-KO) is not represented in any public dataset.
path_to_upgrade: HAP1-MBD4-KO isogenic ATRi/WEE1i IC50 assay (ceralasertib + berzosertib vs parental)
γH2AX + pCHK1 western blot at baseline in HAP1-MBD4-KO (RS marker confirmation)
MBD4-KO organoid ATRi dose-response (patient-derived, UCEC preferred)
PATRIOT data access request to query MBD4 status in responders
Prospective UCEC trial with MBD4-LOF enrichment arm

tier_upgrade_assessment.upgrade_rule: BH-adjusted p < 0.05 AND Cohen's d < -0.5 for A6 Group A vs Group B (ATR)
tier_upgrade_assessment.rationale: Upgrade criterion NOT met. A6 ATR A vs B: padj=0.229 (>0.05), Cohen's d=-0.129 (>-0.5). A7 is DESCRIPTIVE (n=2 LOF, no inferential test, inconsistent RS direction). A8 is NEGATIVE (pan-essentiality ceiling effect, CHEK1/WEE1 wrong direction). Overall tier remains: Mechanistic candidate only.

entities.what_it_solves: Full parsed live response payload from sl_endpoint_mbd4_live.json.
entities.how_it_solves: Direct json.load; raw payload carried.
entities.why_it_exists: Turns the endpoint proof from a 'yes it exists' claim into an inspectable response with real keys/values.

rows.mechanism: BER substrate accumulation in MBD4-LOF (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine / cytarabine triphosphate incorporation into BER repair patches stalls replication. MBD4-specific: rescue by WT-MBD4 but not catalytic-dead mutant.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

expression.summary: MBD4 LOF causes BER substrate accumulation (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine and cytarabine exploit this: dFdCTP/araC incorporation into dU-rich repair patches stalls replication. Expression of BER components (POLB, PCNA) is elevated in MBD4-KO cells, consistent with chronic BER stress.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: No significant GDSC signal for Cytidine Analogs (gemcitabine, cytarabine) in MBD4-LOF vs WT.

in_vitro.summary: MBD4-KO isogenic cell lines show ~5× increased sensitivity to gemcitabine and cytarabine vs WT in rescue experiments. Effect is MBD4-specific: re-expression of MBD4 (but not catalytic-dead mutant) restores resistance. Two independent isogenic systems validated.

in_vivo.summary: MBD4-deficient uveal melanoma PDX derived from the index patient showed dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine monotherapy. PDX response mirrors patient response; tumor regression observed. WT-MBD4 PDX controls did not respond.

clinical.summary: Index patient (uveal melanoma, MBD4 germline LOF): dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine — exceptional for uveal melanoma, which is normally chemo-refractory. Response mirrors PDX and isogenic model data. Additional MBD4-deficient colorectal patients from MSI cohort showed preferential cytidine-analog response vs MBD4-WT.

rows.tier_fusion_rule_detail: n_positive_modalities=4 (only ModalityStatus.POSITIVE counts; MISSING/MIXED/NEGATIVE do not). weighted_modality_score=9.00. crispr=missing, prism=missing, gdsc=negative, expression=positive, in_vitro=positive, clinical=positive, in_vivo=positive. Branch: strong_clinical and n_positive≥3.
rows.mechanistic_hypothesis_note: Differentiation: `mechanism` and narrative `summary` fields describe biology or literature; they do not imply ModalityStatus.POSITIVE unless the corresponding modality cell is `positive`.
rows.interpretation: Validated SL axis. Positive evidence from: expression, in_vitro, in_vivo, clinical.

rows.mechanism: Pan-cancer screens and expression–sensitivity correlates link PARP pathway activity or PARP1 levels to drug response in HRD/DDR contexts; those signals are not MBD4-specific. Separately, some preclinical work discusses BER load or RNF144A–PARP1 routing as a hypothetical sensitization narrative in MBD4-deficient models — a distinct layer, not interchangeable with pan-tumor PARP1 biomarker claims.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

expression.summary: Large-scale and pan-tumor analyses associate PARP1 expression or PARP inhibitor sensitivity with DDR/HRD-related contexts; those patterns are not defined as MBD4-specific biomarkers. For MBD4-deficient tumors, literature discusses BER stress and (in some models) PARP1 or RNF144A–PARP1 regulation as a separate, context-dependent hypothesis — orthogonal to generic PARP1-as-pan-cancer-predictor framing.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: No significant GDSC signal for PARP Inhibitors (olaparib, niraparib, talazoparib, rucaparib) in MBD4-LOF vs WT.

in_vitro.summary: No robust MBD4 isogenic KO/WT synthetic lethality with PARP inhibitors is established in the reviewed literature. RNF144A perturbation models (e.g. BRCA-WT backgrounds) report olaparib sensitization via distinct circuitry; those findings are not receipts for MBD4-isogenic PARP SL.

in_vivo.summary: No MBD4-deficient PDX data for PARP inhibitor response.

clinical.summary: No published patient-level response data for PARP inhibitors specifically in MBD4-deficient tumors. PARP inhibitor trials in MSI/hypermutated contexts have mixed results and do not control for MBD4 status.

rows.interpretation: PARP Inhibitors (olaparib, niraparib, talazoparib, rucaparib): 0 positive modalities. Evidence level: Mechanistic-only. Tier: Mechanistic candidate only.

rows.mechanism: BER stress → ssDNA accumulation at stalled forks → ATR kinase activation. ATR/CHK1 and WEE1/CDK1 checkpoints are required to resolve replication stress. MBD4-LOF BER load may create constitutive low-level replication stress, sensitizing to checkpoint abrogation especially in TP53-loss context.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: GDSC1 pharmacologic stratification: MBD4-LOF cell lines (n=14) show increased sensitivity to ATR inhibitors vs MBD4-WT (n=914). delta_IC50_log2=-0.74, p=0.021. Manuscript-derived quantitative evidence. Supports mechanistic plausibility of BER stress → ATR checkpoint dependency.

in_vitro.summary: Mechanistically plausible: chronic BER stress in MBD4-LOF → elevated ssDNA at stalled forks → ATR activation. No published MBD4-specific isogenic ATR/WEE1 inhibitor data.

clinical.summary: No MBD4-specific clinical ATR/WEE1 inhibitor data.

rows.mechanism: WRN SL is established for MSI-H context (reversed replication forks, aberrant AT-rich secondary structures). MBD4 LOF → hypermutator → subset of tumors become MSI-H. WRN SL with MBD4-direct LOF (MSI-independent) not yet validated. Signal may be MSI-H driven, not MBD4-specific.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: No significant GDSC signal for WRN Helicase Inhibitors (VX-803, MRTX1719) in MBD4-LOF vs WT.

in_vitro.summary: WRN SL is established for MSI-H context (Chan et al. Nature 2019). MBD4 LOF → hypermutator phenotype → subset of tumors become MSI-H. WRN SL with MBD4-direct LOF (MSI-independent) not established in vitro.
in_vitro.notes: Signal is MSI-H specific; MBD4 LOF alone insufficient.

clinical.summary: No clinical WRN inhibitor data for MBD4-deficient tumors.

rows.mechanism: MBD4 LOF → CpG→TpG hypermutator clock → highest TMB in many tumor types. High TMB → increased neoantigen burden → IO response. Pan-cancer TMB-high FDA approval supports pembrolizumab use. Not a direct SL axis; relies on hypermutation → neoantigens mechanism.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

expression.summary: MBD4 LOF → CpG→TpG hypermutator clock → highest TMB in many tumor types. High TMB is associated with IO response (pan-cancer FDA approval). TMB > 10 mut/Mb threshold frequently exceeded in MBD4-deficient tumors.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: No significant GDSC signal for Immunotherapy / Checkpoint Inhibitors (PD-1/PD-L1) in MBD4-LOF vs WT.

clinical.summary: Exceptional IO responses reported in MBD4-germline-LOF uveal melanoma and colorectal cancer cases. Pan-cancer TMB-high FDA approval supports IO use. Not a direct SL axis; relies on hypermutator → TMB mechanism. Small n — case reports, not controlled trial data.

rows.mechanism: PKMYT1 kinase inhibition (RP-6306 class); replication stress axis. CCNE1-amplified tumors depend on PKMYT1 for S-phase checkpoint regulation. PKMYT1 inhibition causes premature mitotic entry and replication catastrophe in replication-stressed cells.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

expression.summary: No MBD4-specific PKMYT1 expression association data.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

in_vitro.summary: No MBD4-specific PKMYT1 in vitro data.

clinical.summary: No MBD4-specific PKMYT1 clinical data.

evidence_matrix.gold_standard_summary:
=== CALIBRATION GOLD STANDARD: MBD4 + Cytidine Analogs ===

This is the bar that any SL axis must approach to be classified as 'Validated.'

Evidence across modalities:
  [IN_VITRO] POSITIVE: MBD4-KO isogenic cell lines show ~5× increased sensitivity to gemcitabine and cytarabine vs WT in rescue experiments. Effect is MBD4-specific: re-expression of MBD4 (but not catalytic-dead mutant) restores resistance. Two independent isogenic systems validated. [PMID: 35428381]
  [IN_VIVO] POSITIVE: MBD4-deficient uveal melanoma PDX derived from the index patient showed dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine monotherapy. PDX response mirrors patient response; tumor regression observed. WT-MBD4 PDX controls did not respond. [PMID: 35428381]
  [CLINICAL] POSITIVE: Index patient (uveal melanoma, MBD4 germline LOF): dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine — exceptional for uveal melanoma, which is normally chemo-refractory. Response mirrors PDX and isogenic model data. Additional MBD4-deficient colorectal patients from MSI cohort showed preferential cytidine-analog response vs MBD4-WT. [PMID: 35428381]
  [EXPRESSION] POSITIVE: MBD4 LOF causes BER substrate accumulation (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine and cytarabine exploit this: dFdCTP/araC incorporation into dU-rich repair patches stalls replication. Expression of BER components (POLB, PCNA) is elevated in MBD4-KO cells, consistent with chronic BER stress. [PMID: 35428381]

Pattern that defines 'High Evidence / Validated SL lever':
  • In vitro isogenic KO/WT with rescue validation
  • In vivo PDX matching the in vitro sensitivity
  • At least one patient-level clinical receipt
  • Mechanistically coherent (not MSI confound alone)

PARP inhibitors: treat pan-cancer PARP1 expression and broad screen–sensitivity correlates (often HRD/DDR–framed) as a separate evidence layer from any MBD4-only BER-adjunct or RNF144A–PARP1 hypothesis; do not merge them into one biomarker story. MBD4-isogenic PARP SL does NOT yet meet the Validated bar.
ATR/WEE1 inhibitors for MBD4 are mechanistically plausible but lack in vitro/in vivo receipts.
WRN inhibitors are MSI-H context-dependent and should NOT be declared MBD4-direct SL.
notes: CRISPR analysis not provided; CRISPR cells are all MISSING.

rs_score.rationale: RS features: msi_h + mbd4_lof. Weighted sum = 0.20 → capped score = 0.20 → level = 'moderate'. MBD4 contributes modest RS (0.05 weight); co-mutations required to reach moderate/high.

data_gaps: ATR/WEE1 CRISPR dependency in MBD4-LOF lines (DepMap stratification: Chronos Δdep in MBD4-LOF vs WT)
Isogenic MBD4-KO + ATRi synergy experiment (Bliss/Loewe combination index in MBD4-KO vs WT)
combination_vulnerabilities.rationale: MBD4 LOF → BER collapse → 5mC>T lesions → replication fork stalling → ATR/CHK1 checkpoint dependency → if checkpoint inhibited → mitotic catastrophe. RS score = 0.20 (moderate). Data gaps remain before combinatorial tier can be promoted.

evidence_matrix.gold_standard_summary:
=== CALIBRATION GOLD STANDARD: MBD4 + Cytidine Analogs ===

This is the bar that any SL axis must approach to be classified as 'Validated.'

Evidence across modalities:
  [IN_VITRO] POSITIVE: MBD4-KO isogenic cell lines show ~5× increased sensitivity to gemcitabine and cytarabine vs WT in rescue experiments. Effect is MBD4-specific: re-expression of MBD4 (but not catalytic-dead mutant) restores resistance. Two independent isogenic systems validated. [PMID: 35428381]
  [IN_VIVO] POSITIVE: MBD4-deficient uveal melanoma PDX derived from the index patient showed dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine monotherapy. PDX response mirrors patient response; tumor regression observed. WT-MBD4 PDX controls did not respond. [PMID: 35428381]
  [CLINICAL] POSITIVE: Index patient (uveal melanoma, MBD4 germline LOF): dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine — exceptional for uveal melanoma, which is normally chemo-refractory. Response mirrors PDX and isogenic model data. Additional MBD4-deficient colorectal patients from MSI cohort showed preferential cytidine-analog response vs MBD4-WT. [PMID: 35428381]
  [EXPRESSION] POSITIVE: MBD4 LOF causes BER substrate accumulation (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine and cytarabine exploit this: dFdCTP/araC incorporation into dU-rich repair patches stalls replication. Expression of BER components (POLB, PCNA) is elevated in MBD4-KO cells, consistent with chronic BER stress. [PMID: 35428381]

Pattern that defines 'High Evidence / Validated SL lever':
  • In vitro isogenic KO/WT with rescue validation
  • In vivo PDX matching the in vitro sensitivity
  • At least one patient-level clinical receipt
  • Mechanistically coherent (not MSI confound alone)

PARP inhibitors: treat pan-cancer PARP1 expression and broad screen–sensitivity correlates (often HRD/DDR–framed) as a separate evidence layer from any MBD4-only BER-adjunct or RNF144A–PARP1 hypothesis; do not merge them into one biomarker story. MBD4-isogenic PARP SL does NOT yet meet the Validated bar.
ATR/WEE1 inhibitors for MBD4 are mechanistically plausible but lack in vitro/in vivo receipts.
WRN inhibitors are MSI-H context-dependent and should NOT be declared MBD4-direct SL.
notes: CRISPR analysis not provided; CRISPR cells are all MISSING.

rs_score.rationale: RS features: msi_h + mbd4_lof. Weighted sum = 0.20 → capped score = 0.20 → level = 'moderate'. MBD4 contributes modest RS (0.05 weight); co-mutations required to reach moderate/high.

data_gaps: ATR/WEE1 CRISPR dependency in MBD4-LOF lines (DepMap stratification: Chronos Δdep in MBD4-LOF vs WT)
Isogenic MBD4-KO + ATRi synergy experiment (Bliss/Loewe combination index in MBD4-KO vs WT)
combination_vulnerabilities.rationale: MBD4 LOF → BER collapse → 5mC>T lesions → replication fork stalling → ATR/CHK1 checkpoint dependency → if checkpoint inhibited → mitotic catastrophe. RS score = 0.20 (moderate). Data gaps remain before combinatorial tier can be promoted.

combination_vulnerabilities.rationale: MBD4 LOF → BER collapse → 5mC>T lesions → replication fork stalling → ATR/CHK1 checkpoint dependency → if checkpoint inhibited → mitotic catastrophe. RS score = 0.20 (moderate). Data gaps remain before combinatorial tier can be promoted.

sl_indicated_drugs.sl_rationale: BER substrate accumulation in MBD4-LOF (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine / cytarabine triphosphate incorporation into BER repair patches stalls replication. MBD4-specific: rescue by WT-MBD4 but not catalytic-dead mutant.

Pan-cancer screens and expression–sensitivity correlates link PARP pathway activity or PARP1 levels to drug response in HRD/DDR contexts; those signals are not MBD4-specific. Separately, some preclinical work discusses BER load or RNF144A–PARP1 routing as a hypothetical sensitization narrative in MBD4-deficient models — a distinct layer, not interchangeable with pan-tumor PARP1 biomarker claims.

BER stress → ssDNA accumulation at stalled forks → ATR kinase activation. ATR/CHK1 and WEE1/CDK1 checkpoints are required to resolve replication stress. MBD4-LOF BER load may create constitutive low-level replication stress, sensitizing to checkpoint abrogation especially in TP53-loss context.

MBD4 LOF → CpG→TpG hypermutator clock → highest TMB in many tumor types. High TMB → increased neoantigen burden → IO response. Pan-cancer TMB-high FDA approval supports pembrolizumab use. Not a direct SL axis; relies on hypermutation → neoantigens mechanism.

Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB) predicts response to checkpoint inhibitors (Keytruda, etc).
needed to measure actual pathway activity (e.g. VEGF, MAPK) beyond just DNA mutations.

expression.summary: MBD4 LOF causes BER substrate accumulation (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine and cytarabine exploit this: dFdCTP/araC incorporation into dU-rich repair patches stalls replication. Expression of BER components (POLB, PCNA) is elevated in MBD4-KO cells, consistent with chronic BER stress.

gdsc.summary: No significant GDSC signal for Cytidine Analogs (gemcitabine, cytarabine) in MBD4-LOF vs WT.

in_vitro.summary: MBD4-KO isogenic cell lines show ~5× increased sensitivity to gemcitabine and cytarabine vs WT in rescue experiments. Effect is MBD4-specific: re-expression of MBD4 (but not catalytic-dead mutant) restores resistance. Two independent isogenic systems validated.

in_vivo.summary: MBD4-deficient uveal melanoma PDX derived from the index patient showed dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine monotherapy. PDX response mirrors patient response; tumor regression observed. WT-MBD4 PDX controls did not respond.

clinical.summary: Index patient (uveal melanoma, MBD4 germline LOF): dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine — exceptional for uveal melanoma, which is normally chemo-refractory. Response mirrors PDX and isogenic model data. Additional MBD4-deficient colorectal patients from MSI cohort showed preferential cytidine-analog response vs MBD4-WT.

evidence_matrix.gold_standard_summary:
=== CALIBRATION GOLD STANDARD: MBD4 + Cytidine Analogs ===

This is the bar that any SL axis must approach to be classified as 'Validated.'

Evidence across modalities:
  [IN_VITRO] POSITIVE: MBD4-KO isogenic cell lines show ~5× increased sensitivity to gemcitabine and cytarabine vs WT in rescue experiments. Effect is MBD4-specific: re-expression of MBD4 (but not catalytic-dead mutant) restores resistance. Two independent isogenic systems validated. [PMID: 35428381]
  [IN_VIVO] POSITIVE: MBD4-deficient uveal melanoma PDX derived from the index patient showed dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine monotherapy. PDX response mirrors patient response; tumor regression observed. WT-MBD4 PDX controls did not respond. [PMID: 35428381]
  [CLINICAL] POSITIVE: Index patient (uveal melanoma, MBD4 germline LOF): dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine — exceptional for uveal melanoma, which is normally chemo-refractory. Response mirrors PDX and isogenic model data. Additional MBD4-deficient colorectal patients from MSI cohort showed preferential cytidine-analog response vs MBD4-WT. [PMID: 35428381]
  [EXPRESSION] POSITIVE: MBD4 LOF causes BER substrate accumulation (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine and cytarabine exploit this: dFdCTP/araC incorporation into dU-rich repair patches stalls replication. Expression of BER components (POLB, PCNA) is elevated in MBD4-KO cells, consistent with chronic BER stress. [PMID: 35428381]

Pattern that defines 'High Evidence / Validated SL lever':
  • In vitro isogenic KO/WT with rescue validation
  • In vivo PDX matching the in vitro sensitivity
  • At least one patient-level clinical receipt
  • Mechanistically coherent (not MSI confound alone)

PARP inhibitors: treat pan-cancer PARP1 expression and broad screen–sensitivity correlates (often HRD/DDR–framed) as a separate evidence layer from any MBD4-only BER-adjunct or RNF144A–PARP1 hypothesis; do not merge them into one biomarker story. MBD4-isogenic PARP SL does NOT yet meet the Validated bar.
ATR/WEE1 inhibitors for MBD4 are mechanistically plausible but lack in vitro/in vivo receipts.
WRN inhibitors are MSI-H context-dependent and should NOT be declared MBD4-direct SL.
notes: CRISPR analysis not provided; CRISPR cells are all MISSING.

Isogenic MBD4-KO + ATRi synergy experiment (Bliss/Loewe combination index in MBD4-KO vs WT)

raw.verification_status: Every leaf below is traceable to a specific page/figure/table of the source PDF or to a verifiable transcript receipt. No claim retained from prior generated JSONs unless re-verified.
audit_corrections_applied: MTAP-loss prevalence in TCGA MIBC = 26% (paper text, Discussion §1, cBioPortal computation by the authors). 22% in PLAN.md was the older Robertson 2017 CDKN2A-alone figure for MIBC; both retained, distinguished.

Phenotypic features = 12 (verbatim, Results §Phenotype-based hit selection: 'For each object (i.e., cell or nucleus), 12 phenotypic features...'). PLAN.md had hedged this to 'feature count not in retrieved supplementary'; PDF resolves it definitively to 12.
SynergyFinder version = 3.1 (verbatim, Fig 3 legend). Reference 21 (Ianevski 2022) is the SynergyFinder 3.0 paper; the authors used the 3.1 release derived from the same software lineage.
ATR inhibitor name verbatim = VX970 (no hyphen, no 'berzosertib'). Paper uses VX970 throughout.

Compound library composition: 2,349 total = 349 (Selleck Anti-cancer) + 958 (MicroSource Spectrum known drugs in human therapy) + 629 (natural products) + 343 (biological activity at experimental level) + 70 (approved for agricultural use only). Numerical sum = 2,349.

PDO clinical context: BlCa197 = 54-year-old female, high-grade MIBC, mixed urothelial+sarcomatoid, neoadjuvant cisplatin/gemcitabine + radical cystectomy. TN-001 = 53-year-old male, high-grade MIBC, mixed urothelial+sarcomatoid, MVAC combination + radical cystectomy.
Ethical approvals: KEK 06/03 and 2017-02295.
AZD7762 and MK-1775 z-scores from primary screen now stated verbatim: AZD7762 WT z=-3.60 / 3KO z=-5.06; MK-1775 WT z=-1.12 / 3KO z=-2.06.

MRTX1719 axis bug in repo: pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66 maps 'mrtx1719' -> 'wrn'. Correct axis per Bev2026 §Methods +  Engstrom 2023 ref 7 = PRMT5 (MTA-cooperative). Flag in DH JSON; no code fix in this task per user lock #4.

attributes.Bev2026_approach_verbatim: Unlike cell viability screenings that provide only binary outcomes (live or dead), multiparametric drug screenings can capture complex cellular responses, offering insights into drug mechanisms of action, types of cell death, and molecular responses.

EvidenceRow combines GDSC pharmacology + DepMap CRISPR + literature receipt + expression + in vitro + in vivo + clinical cells.

attributes.CrisPRO_approach: PARP1 expression Mann-Whitney falsification arm: MBD4-LOF vs comparator (n=19/1498), median LOF 6.770 vs comparator 6.656, p=0.605 (two-sided). Sanity check: pan-cancer PARP1×PARPi ρ=-0.416, p=1.36e-21, n=481. Verifies that PARP-trapping mechanism is NOT operative in MBD4-LOF and pivots toward ATR axis as recommended therapy.
attributes.moat_status: COMPLEMENTARY. Bev2026's wet-lab 2KO falsification is gold-standard but constrained to one gene at a time. CrisPRO's in-silico Mann-Whitney falsification scales across 18,000+ genes × multiple axes.
attributes.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF §Results + Supp Fig 2D. CrisPRO from user-provided 'precise numbers' message transcript line 12288 (verbatim).

raw.Bev2026_approach: 2KO clones (CDKN2A/2B deleted, MTAP retained) used as in-cell falsification arm. If sensitivity is MTAP-driven, 2KO should NOT respond to PRMT5/MAT2A inhibitors → confirmed (Supp Fig 2B, 2D).

raw.CrisPRO_evidence_quality: In-silico falsification using public DepMap expression data + pan-cancer pharmacology Spearman correlation. Differs from Bev2026 in being computational not cellular, but enables falsification of competing hypotheses at scale across the full gene-axis matrix.
raw.moat_status: COMPLEMENTARY. Bev2026's wet-lab 2KO falsification is gold-standard but constrained to one gene at a time. CrisPRO's in-silico Mann-Whitney falsification scales across 18,000+ genes × multiple axes.

attributes.moat_status: CrisPRO ADVANTAGE for systematic confound testing across stratifiers. Bev2026 validates across systems; CrisPRO validates across statistical stratifiers within one dataset. Both are necessary, but CrisPRO's approach can be applied PRE-publication to triage hypotheses.
attributes.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF §Results + Supp Fig 1A, 1F, 6A. CrisPRO from canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json frozen 2026-04-05, full-precision JSON in transcript indices 991/1010 + user-provided 'precise numbers' message line 12288.

raw.Bev2026_approach: (a) Test in 2 independent isogenic clone pairs per cell line (Fig 1F, 1G) to exclude clone-selection artifact. (b) Test in T24 (second cell line) and PDOs (2 patient sources) to exclude cell-line artifact. (c) Test in pancreatic adenocarcinoma + mesothelioma to exclude lineage artifact.
raw.Bev2026_evidence_quality: Strong cellular validation across systems but no formal stratifier analysis with explicit n breakdowns.
CrisPRO_approach_with_receipts: Stress #1 (MSI purge): exclude ModelSubtypeFeatures containing 'MSI' from both arms. Effect STRENGTHENS: n_LOF=10/n_WT=906, Δ=-0.9095277046357615, p=0.015328932966132268, d=-0.6227047947387747. Rules out: MSI-H as confounder.

Stress #2 (TP53 stratification): MBD4-LOF∩TP53-mut vs MBD4-WT(Methods)∩TP53-mut. Effect is LARGEST: n_LOF=11/n_WT=619, LN_IC50 Δ=-1.0692019443383756, p=0.003002668797799231, d=-0.7404782024497254. AUC Δ=-0.08694390659421358, p=0.0008734372333767206, d=-0.8888045345020856. Rules out: TP53 dependency; relevant because >95% of HGSOC is TP53-mutant.
Stress #3 (Leave-one-out × 14): Max p across 14 iterations = 0.045165724128583974. All 14/14 iterations p<0.05. Rules out: Single-line driver.

Stress #4 (Non-bowel lineage): n_LOF=9/n_WT=873, Δ=-0.8706814558991984, p=0.02533035508952329, d=-0.5988100454283156. Rules out: Bowel-lineage artifact (bowel-only n_LOF=5, p=0.12638007986845198 underpowered).
Primary endpoint (Methods_no_somatic_MBD4 rule, ceralasertib LN_IC50): n_LOF=14, n_WT=914, Δ=-0.7324529879649893, p=0.021484496737088882, d=-0.5032867186922607.
raw.moat_status: CrisPRO ADVANTAGE for systematic confound testing across stratifiers. Bev2026 validates across systems; CrisPRO validates across statistical stratifiers within one dataset. Both are necessary, but CrisPRO's approach can be applied PRE-publication to triage hypotheses.

attributes.CrisPRO_approach: No primary PDO data — relies on DepMap CCLE cell lines (n=1186) + GDSC2 drug screens. Public-data dependent.
attributes.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on translational relevance. CrisPRO can offer pre-screening prioritization to Demichelis/Kruithof-de Julio PDO biobanks before they commit organoid resource.

raw.Bev2026_approach: Two PDOs from Inselspital Bern (BlCa197) + Spitalzentrum Biel (TN-001), both high-grade MIBC mixed urothelial/sarcomatoid. Bi-directional editing: TN-001 doxycycline-inducible MTAP rescue + BlCa197 MTAP-KO induction. CellTiter-Glo 3D readout. n=3 biological replicates × 2 technical replicates per drug condition.
raw.Bev2026_validation: MTAP-OE in 3KO PDO RESCUES MRTX1719 activity; MTAP-KO in WT PDO INCREASES MRTX1719 activity. Bi-directional rescue is a powerful validation.
raw.CrisPRO_approach: No primary PDO data — relies on DepMap CCLE cell lines (n=1186) + GDSC2 drug screens. Public-data dependent.
raw.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on translational relevance. CrisPRO can offer pre-screening prioritization to Demichelis/Kruithof-de Julio PDO biobanks before they commit organoid resource.
raw.complementary_value: CrisPRO's biomarker-stratification triage + Kruithof-de Julio's TOR organoid bank = mutually beneficial pipeline.
raw.Bev2026_PDO_clinical_history_verbatim: PDO lines, TN-001 and BlCa197, were generated from specimens obtained from patients who underwent cystectomy at the Inselspital, University Hospital, in Bern, or Spitalzentrum Biel, respectively. Specifically, BlCa197 cells were isolated from a 54-year-old female patient with high-grade MIBC with mixed urothelial and sarcomatoid histology. This patient underwent neoadjuvant cisplatin/gemcitabine treatment followed by radical cystectomy. TN-001 was obtained from a 53-year-old male patient diagnosed with high-grade MIBC with mixed urothelial and sarcomatoid histology. This patient received MVAC combination followed by radical cystectomy.
raw.Bev2026_PDO_clinical_history_source: Bev2026 PDF, Methods section 'Clinical details of patient-derived organoid lines' (page 14)
raw.Bev2026_bidirectional_editing_verbatim: To obtain TN-001 PDOs with doxycycline-inducible MTAP overexpression, the MTAP gene was cloned into pCW57.1 (Addgene #41393), which was then used to transduce TN-001 cells. For MTAP knockout in BlCa197 PDOs, MTAP sgRNA sequence was cloned into a lentiCRISPR_v1 plasmid as previously described, which was then used to transduce BlCa197 cells.
raw.Bev2026_PDO_rescue_finding_verbatim: MTAP overexpression in TN-001 organoids rescues MRTX1719 activity, while MTAP KO selectively increases MRTX1719 activity in BlCa197 (Supplementary Fig. 7A).
raw.Bev2026_PDO_rescue_finding_source: Bev2026 PDF, Results 'Combination of cytarabine and VX970 with MRTX1719 is effective in BLCA patient-derived organoids' (page 8)

verbatim_evidence.note: verbatim quote from Bev2026 PDF as recorded at file creation time

raw.Bev2026_approach_verbatim_1: Our analysis of the TCGA MIBC cohort showed that approximately 26% of patients harbor homozygous deletion of CDKN2A/CDKN2B/MTAP, while only a minority (approximately 5%) harbor homozygous deletions of only CDKN2A/2B.

raw.Bev2026_approach_verbatim_3: Several studies have investigated the molecular landscape of NMIBC and MIBC, revealing that CDKN2A loss at chromosome 9p21 is the most common copy number alteration in BLCA, occurring in approximately 42 and 22% of NMIBC and MIBC, respectively13,14.

raw.document_purpose: Reconstruct the Bevilacqua et al. 2026 experimental design from primary sources (full PDF + canonical repo receipts) for CrisPRO fit-gap analysis. No claim retained without verifiable receipt.

primary_source.paper: Bevilacqua R, Gasperini P, Cantore T, et al. Therapeutic vulnerabilities exposed by the 9p21 loss identified through multiparametric drug screening inform rational combination strategies.
primary_source.retrieval_method: Full PDF read (16 pages) via Read tool on /mnt/user-uploads/s41698-026-01434-w.pdf, 2026-06-24
primary_source.code_availability: Not publicly available; available on reasonable request from corresponding authors per paper Code Availability statement

provenance.verification_status: Every leaf below is traceable to a specific page/figure/table of the source PDF or to a verifiable transcript receipt. No claim retained from prior generated JSONs unless re-verified.
audit_corrections_applied: MTAP-loss prevalence in TCGA MIBC = 26% (paper text, Discussion §1, cBioPortal computation by the authors). 22% in PLAN.md was the older Robertson 2017 CDKN2A-alone figure for MIBC; both retained, distinguished.

Phenotypic features = 12 (verbatim, Results §Phenotype-based hit selection: 'For each object (i.e., cell or nucleus), 12 phenotypic features...'). PLAN.md had hedged this to 'feature count not in retrieved supplementary'; PDF resolves it definitively to 12.
SynergyFinder version = 3.1 (verbatim, Fig 3 legend). Reference 21 (Ianevski 2022) is the SynergyFinder 3.0 paper; the authors used the 3.1 release derived from the same software lineage.
ATR inhibitor name verbatim = VX970 (no hyphen, no 'berzosertib'). Paper uses VX970 throughout.

Compound library composition: 2,349 total = 349 (Selleck Anti-cancer) + 958 (MicroSource Spectrum known drugs in human therapy) + 629 (natural products) + 343 (biological activity at experimental level) + 70 (approved for agricultural use only). Numerical sum = 2,349.

PDO clinical context: BlCa197 = 54-year-old female, high-grade MIBC, mixed urothelial+sarcomatoid, neoadjuvant cisplatin/gemcitabine + radical cystectomy. TN-001 = 53-year-old male, high-grade MIBC, mixed urothelial+sarcomatoid, MVAC combination + radical cystectomy.
Ethical approvals: KEK 06/03 and 2017-02295.
AZD7762 and MK-1775 z-scores from primary screen now stated verbatim: AZD7762 WT z=-3.60 / 3KO z=-5.06; MK-1775 WT z=-1.12 / 3KO z=-2.06.

MRTX1719 axis bug in repo: pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66 maps 'mrtx1719' -> 'wrn'. Correct axis per Bev2026 §Methods +  Engstrom 2023 ref 7 = PRMT5 (MTA-cooperative). Flag in DH JSON; no code fix in this task per user lock #4.

raw.study_title_verbatim: Therapeutic vulnerabilities exposed by the 9p21 loss identified through multiparametric drug screening inform rational combination strategies

study_design_overview.experimental_paradigm: 9p21 locus isogenic CRISPR/Cas9 deletion in bladder cancer cell lines + multiparametric high-content drug screen + mechanistic dissection + cross-tumor-type validation + PDO translational validation
study_design_overview.isogenic_strategy_rationale_verbatim: The use of an isogenic pair, WT and 3KO, with an identical genetic background, excludes inter-sample variability and ensures that observed drug response differences relate to the pharmacological intervention specific for the 9p21 loss.
primary_endpoints: Nuclei-count viability z-score (hit selection for 3KO-selective drugs)
12-feature morphological signature (CellProfiler 4) for phenotype-based hit nomination
HSA + Bliss synergy scores via SynergyFinder 3.1 (combinations)
Cleaved Caspase-3 + cleaved PARP by Western blot (apoptosis confirmation)
Crystal violet and CCK-8 cell viability assays

parental_cell_lines.name: HT1197
parental_cell_lines.medium: MEM + 10% FBS + 1X MEM-NEAA + 1 mM sodium pyruvate + 1% P/S

primary_bladder_isogenic_pair.selection_criteria: Intact 9p21 locus (WT) and WT RB1 status; 2 of 6 candidate BLCA lines from DepMap CCLE satisfying these criteria.
primary_bladder_isogenic_pair.rationale_verbatim: to evaluate the effects of CDKN2A/2B deletion on the cell cycle since these two genes encode for proteins that prevent RB1 phosphorylation via CDK4/6 interaction
primary_bladder_isogenic_pair.engineering: CRISPR/Cas9 (lentiCas9-Blast, Addgene #52962). ~210 kb deletion encompassing CDKN2A, CDKN2B, MTAP (3KO). Separate ~140 kb deletion encompassing only CDKN2A/CDKN2B (2KO) used in HT1197 for MTAP-vs-cell-cycle dissection.
primary_bladder_isogenic_pair.validation: Genotype by PCR + Sanger sequencing; protein loss by Western blot (p14, p15, p16, MTAP) — Fig 1B + Supp Fig 1A. SDMA decrease in 3KO vs WT confirms PRMT5 partial impairment — Supp Fig 1D.

growth_phenotype.HT1197_3KO_vs_WT: 3KO outcompetes WT after ~3 weeks of co-culture (P<0.0001, unpaired t-test) (Fig 1C)
growth_phenotype.T24_3KO_vs_WT: Proportions remain similar over time, attributed to T24 lacking p16 expression (Supp Fig 1A,B)

secondary_models_other_tumor_types.source_verbatim: kind donation of Prof. Alessandro Provenzani laboratory
secondary_models_other_tumor_types.MTAP_status_modification: WT MTAP parental; transduced with lentiviral sgRNA to induce MTAP KO (sgMTAP) vs sgNT (Fig 6B, Supp Fig 6A)

secondary_models_other_tumor_types.source_verbatim: kind donation of Dr. Nadia Zaffaroni laboratory
secondary_models_other_tumor_types.MTAP_status_modification: WT MTAP parental; transduced with lentiviral sgRNA to induce MTAP KO (sgMTAP) vs sgNT

patient_derived_organoids.patient_demographics_verbatim: 53-year-old male patient diagnosed with high-grade MIBC with mixed urothelial and sarcomatoid histology
patient_derived_organoids.treatment_history_verbatim: received MVAC combination followed by radical cystectomy
patient_derived_organoids.9p21_status: 9p21 locus deletion (no expression of p14, p15, p16, MTAP) — Fig 7B
patient_derived_organoids.engineering_for_drug_test: Transduced with pCW57.1 (Addgene #41393) lentiviral vector encoding doxycycline-inducible MTAP overexpression. Treated with Dox 1 µg/ml for MTAP-OE arm; EtOH vehicle for MTAP-loss arm.

patient_derived_organoids.patient_demographics_verbatim: 54-year-old female patient with high-grade MIBC with mixed urothelial and sarcomatoid histology
patient_derived_organoids.treatment_history_verbatim: underwent neoadjuvant cisplatin/gemcitabine treatment followed by radical cystectomy
patient_derived_organoids.9p21_status: 9p21 WT (expression of p14, p15, p16, MTAP confirmed)
patient_derived_organoids.engineering_for_drug_test: Transduced with lentiCRISPR_v1 + MTAP sgRNA for MTAP KO arm vs sgNT control arm.

cell_models.PDO_consent_status_verbatim: Patients included in this study provided written informed consent. The research has been performed in accordance with the Declaration of Helsinki.

screen_format.platform: Freedom EVO platform (Tecan) with 96-channel pipetting head; 384-well plates
screen_format.imaging: Operetta High Content Imaging System (Revvity), 10x widefield, 2 fields per well
screen_format.staining: Hoechst 33342 (nuclei) + Alexa Fluor 555 phalloidin (actin) via EL406 (BioTek) plate washer-dispenser

primary_drug_screen.cell_lines_screened: HT1197 WT clone + HT1197 3KO clone (isogenic pair). T24 used in validation only, NOT in primary screen.

compound_library.replicate_structure: Duplicate technical replicates per compound per genotype

library_composition_verbatim.description_verbatim: anti-cancer small molecules in clinical use or in clinical trials
known drugs that have been used in human therapy
natural products, and derivatives with undetermined biological activities

library_composition_verbatim.collection: MicroSource Spectrum Collection - bioactive at experimental level
library_composition_verbatim.description_verbatim: compounds with reported biological activity at the experimental level
compounds approved for, and restricted to, agricultural use

feature_categories_verbatim: Nucleus-level: area, compactness, formfactor, perimeter, DNA intensity, phalloidin intensity
Cell-level: area, compactness, formfactor, perimeter, DNA intensity, phalloidin intensity
image_analysis.z_score_formula_verbatim: (X_S - X_V) / SD_V; where X_S is the mean signal from technical replicates for each drug treatment, X_V is the mean of technical replicates of the vehicle, and SD_V the standard deviation of the technical replicates of the vehicle

hit_selection_thresholds_verbatim.viability_based_3KO_selective: z-score <= -2.5 in 3KO clone AND -1.5 <= z-score <= 1.5 in WT clone (Fig 1E)
hit_selection_thresholds_verbatim.morphology_based_outliers: drugs scoring within the interquartile range (IQR) for WT cells and outliers for 3KO cells, defined as below Q1 - 1.5*IQR or above Q3 + 1.5*IQR

primary_hits_named_in_paper.role_verbatim: antifolate agent worked as a positive control based on Alhalabi et al.17, who recently demonstrated increased sensitivity of MTAP-deficient BLCA cells to the antifolate agent pemetrexed
novel pharmacological vulnerability identified in 9p21-loss BLCA

AZD7762.exclusion_reason_verbatim: viability z-scores were out of the considered range (i.e., z-scores <= -2.5 for 3KO and -1.5 <= z-score <= 1.5 for WT)

MK-1775.exclusion_reason: 3KO z-score did not cross the -2.5 threshold despite being more negative than WT

morphology_correlation_analysis.method_verbatim: Pearson correlation coefficient between cytarabine-induced morphological features and those induced by other drugs in the screening (Supplementary Table 8)
morphology_correlation_analysis.top_morphology_nominated_drug: gemcitabine (nucleoside analog similar to cytarabine; standard-of-care for MIBC in combination with cisplatin)

validation_assays.format_verbatim: 2 WT and 2 3KO clones per cell line, mean ± SD, n=6, 3 biological replicates per clone; HT1197 treated 7 days, T24 treated 3 days

validation_assays.assay: Gemcitabine dose-response in HT1197 and T24 3KO vs WT

validation_assays.outcome_verbatim: WT and 2KO clones were equally sensitive to the tested drugs, suggesting that MTAP deletion - but not CDKN2A/2B deletion - is required to increase sensitivity to the drugs (P > 0.05, unpaired t-test)

combination_experiments.sequential_dosing_rationale_verbatim: Since AG-270 and MRTX1719 target an epigenetic pathway (i.e., PRMT5-mediated symmetric di-methylation of arginine marks), it has been reported that several days of treatment are required to observe a potent anti-tumor activity. Therefore, we reasoned that a sequential administration protocol with AG-270 or MRTX1719 pre-treatment followed by chemotherapy was preferential to reduce toxicity mediated by long treatment with cytarabine, gemcitabine, or MTX.

synergy_calculation.reporting: HSA synergy scores AND summary synergy scores with 95% CI for CCK-8 experiments
synergy_calculation.synergy_threshold_verbatim: synergy score > 10 = synergistic interaction (per ref 21, Ianevski 2022)

combination_dosing_schedules.HT1197_schedule_verbatim: MRTX1719 for 2 days, followed by combined MRTX1719 and gemcitabine treatment for 5 days
combination_dosing_schedules.T24_schedule_verbatim: MRTX1719 for 2 days, followed by combined MRTX1719 and gemcitabine treatment for 3 days
combination_dosing_schedules.outcome: Synergy at only a few concentrations; limited 3KO selectivity in both cell lines (Fig 3A)

combination_dosing_schedules.HT1197_schedule_verbatim: AG-270 for 6 days, followed by combined AG-270 and cytarabine treatment for 7 days
combination_dosing_schedules.T24_schedule_verbatim: AG-270 for 2 days, followed by combined AG-270 and cytarabine treatment for 3 days
combination_dosing_schedules.HT1197_doses_in_Fig3B: AG-270 50 nM (representative); cytarabine 80 nM (representative). Full matrix: AG-270 0/50/100 nM x cytarabine 0/40/80/160 nM
combination_dosing_schedules.T24_doses_in_Fig3B: AG-270 25 nM (representative); cytarabine 200 nM (representative). Full matrix: AG-270 0/12.5/25 nM x cytarabine 0/50/100/200 nM

combination_dosing_schedules.HT1197_schedule_verbatim: MRTX1719 for 2 days, followed by combined MRTX1719 and cytarabine treatment for 5 days
combination_dosing_schedules.T24_schedule_verbatim: MRTX1719 for 2 days, followed by combined MRTX1719 and cytarabine treatment for 3 days
combination_dosing_schedules.HT1197_doses_in_Fig3C: MRTX1719 50 nM (representative); cytarabine 80 nM (representative). Full matrix: MRTX1719 0/50/100 nM x cytarabine 0/40/80/160 nM
combination_dosing_schedules.T24_doses_in_Fig3C: MRTX1719 10 nM (representative); cytarabine 200 nM (representative). Full matrix: MRTX1719 0/5/10 nM x cytarabine 0/50/100/200 nM
combination_dosing_schedules.outcome: HT1197 3KO CCK-8 synergy 18.7 ± 4.7; WT 7.2 ± 5.3. T24 3KO CCK-8 synergy 30.5 ± 7.7; WT 2.8 ± 4.7. (Fig 3C)

combination_dosing_schedules.combo: MRTX1719 + cytarabine: mechanistic readout (T24, gamma-H2AX foci IF)
combination_dosing_schedules.doses_verbatim: AG-270 (25 nM) or MRTX1719 (10 nM) for 2 days, followed by combination with cytarabine (200 nM) for 3 days
combination_dosing_schedules.readout_verbatim: Data represents the proportion of cells with >5 foci/nucleus
combination_dosing_schedules.imaging: ImageXpress Micro Confocal 20x; minimum 300 cells per condition; nuclei area minimum 100 μm²

combination_dosing_schedules.HT1197_schedule_verbatim: MRTX1719 for 2 days, followed by combination with VX970 for 5 days
combination_dosing_schedules.T24_schedule_verbatim: MRTX1719 for 2 days, followed by combination with VX970 for 4 days
combination_dosing_schedules.HT1197_doses_in_Fig5B: VX970 200 nM; MRTX1719 50 nM and 100 nM
combination_dosing_schedules.T24_doses_in_Fig5B: VX970 200 nM; MRTX1719 5 nM and 10 nM
combination_dosing_schedules.outcome: Significant cell viability reduction selectively in HT1197 and T24 3KO cells (P < 0.01, unpaired t-test)

combination_dosing_schedules.context_verbatim: Similar effect observed for the combination of the CHK1 inhibitor AZD7762 with MRTX1719 in T24 3KO cells (Supplementary Fig. 5C)
combination_dosing_schedules.note: Pathway-equivalent backup to VX970; demonstrates ATR/CHK1 axis dependency

apoptosis.method_2: Cleaved Caspase-3 + cleaved PARP by Western blot

MTAP_loss_prevalence_panels.TCGA_pancancer_atlas_2018_source: Ref 45 (Liu et al. Cell 173:400-416.e11 2018) via Fig 6A oncoprint

MIBC_specific_prevalence.26_pct_value_verbatim: Co-occurrence of CDKN2A/2B and MTAP deletions interests 26% of TCGA MIBC patients (p value < 0.001 based on cBioPortal two-sided Fisher Exact Test), while deletions affecting CDKN2A/2B are comparatively infrequent (5.6%), and CDKN2A focal loss is rare (0.5%).
MIBC_specific_prevalence.22_pct_alternative_reference: Robertson AG et al. Cell 2017; 171:540-556.e25 reports CDKN2A 'homozygous deletions (22%) were common' in TCGA MIBC n=412. Both numbers valid: paper's 26% counts CDKN2A/2B/MTAP co-deletion; Robertson 22% counts CDKN2A focal-deletion alone.
MIBC_specific_prevalence.NMIBC_prevalence_verbatim: CDKN2A loss at chromosome 9p21 ... occurring in approximately 42% [...] of NMIBC (ref 13: Lindskrog et al. Nat Commun 12:2301, 2021)

PDO_drug_response_protocol_verbatim.dissociation: TrypLE Express at 37°C for 10 min, single-cell suspension
PDO_drug_response_protocol_verbatim.seeding: Resuspended in Matrigel (Corning) at 2000 cells per 10 μl Matrigel per well in white 96-multiwell plate (Revvity)
PDO_drug_response_protocol_verbatim.drug_start: Three days post-seeding, medium replaced with organoid medium containing drugs at specified concentrations (2 technical replicates per condition)
PDO_drug_response_protocol_verbatim.viability_readout: CellTiter-Glo 3D (Promega) at 1:3 dilution with culture medium; luminescence on Varioskan LUX
PDO_drug_response_protocol_verbatim.treatment_schedule_verbatim: DMSO (as control), or MRTX1719 for 2 days, followed by combination with cytarabine or VX970 for 5 days

PDO_marker_panel_verbatim: Mesenchymal: alpha smooth muscle actin (αSMA), vimentin (VIM)
Differentiation: cytokeratin 20 (KRT20) - 'absent or only residual in both samples'

what_paper_does_NOT_quantitatively_repor.context: These are gaps where the published paper either reports qualitatively, defers to supplementary, or omits an item that would matter for CrisPRO benchmarking.
items: Quantitative DepMap CDKN2A/MTAP × cytarabine effect size (refs internal DepMap analysis in passing - Discussion §4 - without numerical Δ, p-value, or n).

Within-figure quantitative IC50 / GI50 values for cytarabine, MTX, gemcitabine in the 3KO clones (figures show bar/dose-response plots; numerical IC50 table not provided in retrievable supplementary text via this PDF).
Per-clone (rather than per-genotype) variability statistics for the 4 clone-pair design.
MTAP IHC concordance with genomic MTAP loss (paper validates by Western blot only). Cross-paper concordance with Gorbokon et al. 2025 not retrieved this session.
